The real defect is narrower and more interesting than the one I first announced: not "never works" but "**silently degrades to zero exactly when it matters most**." *The lesson isn't new, it's that I keep needing it: check the surface the writer actually writes to, before saying a thing never happened.*

**On the process half of the user's question.** Durable findings DID reach the kit this session (`P-XMSVZWDD`, `P-ENJNBQ7N`, `P-RYN7KWJJ`, `P-aKTA2KND`) — but D-367's and D-368's went only to docs until the user asked, and were then captured retroactively (`P-B3BLXE9V`, `P-KJ32WCUR`, `P-RP97AHKK`, `P-U5LLL2CL`). With the automatic path starved, the manual `cmk remember` at each finding **was** the capture path, and I used it inconsistently — filing to `tasks.md` and treating that as done. Task 242 fixes the machine half; the discipline half is the existing per-task-boundary dogfood flush (CLAUDE.md checkpoint pattern #4), which says the same thing and which I did not honor per-finding.

---

## 2026-07-20 — D-368: NOTE — measured clone audit over all 125 src modules: the fact-store walk is duplicated 9-10× with no shared walker (Task 241); `slugify` is a trap, not a dupe

**Trigger.** The user asked whether there is more code duplication worth removing. Measured with a normalized sliding-window hash scan over every `packages/cli/src/*.mjs` (comments/blank/brace-only lines stripped, windows hashed, cross-file collisions reported longest-first), then **every candidate read** — a scanner finds *structural* similarity; only reading separates "same code" from "same shape, different contract."

**Numbers:** 38 cross-file clone groups at an 8-line window; **2 survive at 14 lines**. Duplicate helper NAMES defined in 2+ files: 17.

**The real finding — the fact-store walk has no shared home.** 10 modules walk `resolveFactDir` with `readdirSync`; the skip-idiom `entry.name === 'INDEX.md'` appears in **9 files**; `listLiveFactFiles`/`listFactFiles` is the **same byte-identical body under two names in 4 files** (forget / merge-facts / reindex / trust); and `temporal-sweep.mjs:69-93` ↔ `validity-window.mjs:42-65` is a **14-line** walk-parse-skip clone differing only in its final predicate. Meanwhile `tier-paths.mjs` already exports `resolveFactDir` — the natural home was there the whole time. **This is the exact class the CLAUDE.md shared-modules table was written for** (the Layer-2 review that found 4 modules reimplementing the same helpers, with drift already producing bugs — INDEX.md unfiltered in one writer's dedup scan). A new skip rule today must be remembered in 9 places. → **Task 241 (v0.6.2)**, with `escapeRegExp` (2 sites, byte-identical), `asIsoString` (2 sites, byte-identical — and `validity-window`'s own comment admits *"same as expiry-sweep.mjs"*), and `dateFromIso` (3 sites, functionally identical) as riders.

**The one worth naming — `slugify` is a TRAP.** Three sites, one name, and the scanner flags it. Reading them: `graduation.mjs` caps at **40** with a deliberately ReDoS-safe string-op shape; `judgment.mjs` caps at **60** via a regex trim; `rich-fact.mjs::slugifyFact` caps at **60** with a `'fact'` fallback. Merging them **silently changes generated filenames across three subsystems.** A mechanical de-dupe here is a data bug wearing a refactor's clothes. Recorded in Task 241's entry as explicitly-rejected so nobody re-proposes it from the same scan output.

**Also rejected, deliberately:** the `MISSING_PROJECT_ROOT`/`MISSING_BACKEND` guard preamble (11 + 8 sites) — genuinely repetitive, but it is entry-point *contract* that reads better inline, and each site computes `duration_ms` from its own `t0`; and `validateOptions`/`readSettings`/`findSectionRange` (3 sites each) — same name, different per-module contracts, not verified identical, so out of scope rather than assumed.

**Method note worth keeping.** Of the candidates the scanner surfaced, the highest-count one (`slugify`, 3 sites) was the one that must NOT be touched, and the most valuable one (the fact-walk) only became visible after *counting the idiom across files* rather than trusting the window hash. **Clone detection proposes; reading disposes.** This is the third measure-don't-assume pass this session (D-366 retraction, D-367 sweep, this) — the pattern holds: the check is cheap, the assumption is expensive.

---

## 2026-07-20 — D-367: DECISION — the ECC process sweep: 1 of 4 borrows ACCEPTED (Node-pin drift, Task 240), 3 REJECTED on evidence

**Trigger.** The user asked whether the three additional ECC process observations I'd named in chat were tasks. They were not — they were unverified chat claims, the exact state the "actionable tasks, laned to real versions" directive (D-364 lane work) forbids. So each got checked against our own repo before being written down. **Three of the four died on contact with evidence**, which is the point of checking.

**ACCEPTED → Task 240 (v0.6.2): CI toolchain drift.** Measured: `node-version` is a copy-pasted literal in **9 `setup-node` blocks across 8 workflows** (`ci.yml` alone has 3), and they **already disagree** — `bench-storage.yml` pins **24**, everything else pins **20**. No `.nvmrc` / `.tool-versions` / shared constant exists; `package.json` declares only the `">=20"` consumer floor. A benchmark job running a different major than the gates that approve the code is the composition-verification class applied to CI (a gate must run the runtime the release runs), and the drift is *already present*, not hypothetical. Fix: one `.nvmrc` + `node-version-file:` everywhere, a deliberate documented decision for the bench job, and a guard so it cannot silently re-drift.

**REJECTED — commitlint.** ECC ships `commitlint.config.js`; we do not, and our commit conventions live as prose in CLAUDE.md, so this *looked* like a textbook prose→enforcement graduation. Measured instead: **38 of the last 40 non-merge commits already conform**, and both outliers use `research(recall):` — a scope we chose deliberately. A stock conventional-commits linter would flag *our own convention* as an error. Enforcement for a discipline that is already 95% self-held, at the cost of fighting the tool over a scope we want, is negative value.

**REJECTED — a `TROUBLESHOOTING.md`.** ECC separates "what people actually hit" from "what the diagnostic reports"; I proposed mirroring it. Measured: [`QUICKSTART.md`](../../QUICKSTART.md) §Troubleshooting (line ~185) **already carries a Symptom / Likely cause / Fix table**. Adding the file would create a *second surface for one concern* — precisely the rogue-doc-surface class `validate-doc-registry` + DOCUMENTATION-MAP exist to prevent. The right move if that table outgrows QUICKSTART is to *move* it, not to duplicate it.

**REJECTED — reusable `workflow_call` files.** The stated benefit was de-duplicating CI setup. Measured: the real duplication IS the 9 `setup-node` blocks, which `.nvmrc` fixes in one line each. Extracting reusable workflows to solve that is ceremony over a one-line fix (the over-engineering anti-pattern), and it would add an indirection layer to the release path — the surface where we least want indirection.

**The meta-point worth keeping.** Four plausible-sounding borrows from a 211k-star project; **one survived a five-minute check of our own repo.** "They do X and we don't" is a hypothesis, not a finding — the gap is only real if our side actually lacks it, and three times out of four here, we didn't. The rejections are recorded explicitly (in Task 240's entry too) so nobody re-proposes them from the same surface reading.

---

## 2026-07-20 — D-366: RETRACTION — D-365's "data-loss bug" DOES NOT EXIST; the probe was wrong, not the kit

**Retracts the BUG half of [D-365](#2026-07-20--d-365-bug--concurrent-scratchpad-writes-silently-lose-bullets-the-no-parallel-agent-surface-premise-was-disproved-by-a-20-line-probe) in full.** D-365 is kept below (decision-trail rule) but its core claim is **false**. Task 239 is **CLOSED as not-a-bug**; the `file-lock.mjs` + wrapped critical section written to "fix" it were reverted unmerged.

**Ground truth (16 SERIAL writes, every record accounted for across the whole tier):** all 16 bullets are present. 8 remain in `MEMORY.md`; the other 8 **graduated** into individual fact files under `context/memory/` — each with its own file, an `INDEX.md` entry, and full searchability. **Zero loss.** This is `graduateForCapRelief` working exactly as designed (design §19 load-cap-not-write-cap, §20.3 P-tier graduation): when the hot index hits its cap, the oldest high-trust bullets move to the permanent fact store rather than being dropped.

**What the probe actually measured.** It counted `MEMORY.md` occurrences and called the difference LOST — never checking `context/memory/`, `archive/evicted-bullets.md`, or the audit log (which names every evicted/graduated id **with its destination path**). A tier where cap pressure deliberately MOVES bullets between surfaces cannot be audited by grepping one surface.

**The falsifying test I should have run first.** Serial: 9/16 in MEMORY.md. Staggered (150ms apart): 9/16. Parallel: 9/16. **Identical with zero concurrency** — and a lost-update race cannot reproduce without concurrency. One serial baseline run would have killed the diagnosis in a minute; I ran it only after a lock trace (16 perfectly-serialized ACQUIRE/RELEASE pairs, no overlap) proved the lock was working while bullets still "vanished."

**The error class (twice in one session).** D-364 §6 logged me claiming ECC shipped Task-95 re-curation from an architecture *diagram* instead of code. This is the same failure with a different input: a symptom that **resembled** a known bug class (concurrent writes + missing rows + exit 0 = lost update) accepted as diagnosis without the one measurement that distinguishes it. Resemblance is not evidence. The irony is exact — I found ECC's unimplemented confidence-evolution by checking whether the code did what the docs said, then asserted a race without checking whether the data was actually gone.

**Binding takeaway (added to the verification discipline):** before reporting ANY data-loss bug — (1) **run the serial baseline FIRST**; if the loss reproduces with zero concurrency it is not a race; (2) **account for every record across the WHOLE tier** (`memory/` fact files, `archive/evicted-bullets.md`, `archive/superseded/`, `tombstones/`) before using the word "lost" — this kit MOVES records by design; (3) **read the audit log**, which already names each moved id and its destination. Captured as `P-aKTA2KND`.

**What SURVIVES from D-365 (still true + still valuable):** the *user's* point that a cheaply-testable premise must be TESTED, not asserted. That half was right and produced real work — it is now recorded as the meta-rule below, and it is what eventually falsified my own claim too. Task 238's premise correction also stands on its own evidence (the parallel surface IS reachable via workflow fan-out / two windows / Claude+Kiro); only the "and it corrupts memory" conclusion is withdrawn.

---

## 2026-07-20 — D-365: BUG — concurrent scratchpad writes silently lose bullets; the "no parallel-agent surface" premise was disproved by a 20-line probe

> **⚠️ RETRACTED 2026-07-20 by [D-366](#2026-07-20--d-366-retraction--d-365s-data-loss-bug-does-not-exist-the-probe-was-wrong-not-the-kit) — the data-loss claim below is FALSE.** All bullets were accounted for (8 in `MEMORY.md`, 8 graduated to `context/memory/` fact files); the probe grepped one surface of a tier that moves records by design. Kept unedited per the decision-trail rule. The surviving true half is the meta-lesson at the end: a cheaply-testable premise must be probed, not asserted.

**Trigger.** Immediately after D-364 laned Task 238 as *not-laned, no problem yet*, the user asked: *"about 'parallel-agent surface' — can't you test that? run the next task with agents like if I enable ultracode+workflow and see what happens?"* The question was better than my reasoning: I had asserted the trigger hadn't fired without ever **probing** whether it would.

**The probe (real repo binary, sandboxed tiers, Windows).** N concurrent `cmk remember` processes against ONE project tier — the exact write path a workflow fan-out's subagent Stop hooks / `mk_remember` calls exercise.

- **Rich fact writes: CLEAN.** 8-way and 24-way → 24/24 facts on disk, INDEX complete, zero leaked locks, zero duplicates. Each fact is its own file; no contention.
- **Shared-scratchpad writes: BROKEN.** 16-way → **8 of 16 bullets LOST**, **all 16 processes exit 0 reporting success**, zero leaked locks, file structurally intact (headings + comment markers balanced). Reproduced twice with *different victim sets*. **8-way was clean** → load-threshold dependent, which is why no test or live-test ever caught it.

**Root cause.** `appendScratchpadBullet` (`scratchpad.mjs:362`) is an **unguarded** `readFileSync` → mutate → `writeFileSync`: no lock, no atomic rename, no compare-and-swap. Two processes read the same `original`; the second write clobbers the first. Same shape at the `memory-write.mjs` remove (471/496) and replace (580/615) sites. **The irony:** `.locks/` + `lock-discipline.mjs` ship specifically to DETECT stale locks, while the scratchpad write path never TAKES one — a detection half with no acquisition half.

**Why it matters.** Silent data loss behind a success exit code, in the subsystem whose entire promise is durability. And it is **reachable on shipped configs today**, not hypothetically: a workflow/ultracode fan-out, two Claude Code windows on one repo, or the **supported** Claude + Kiro dual-agent setup.

**→ Task 239, laned v0.6.0 (the current cut).** Fix in the write path (exclusive `O_EXCL` lock reusing the shipped stale-lock detection, or temp-write + atomic rename with a content-hash CAS retry — decided on evidence); applied to ALL sibling read-modify-write sites via a both-ways caller map; loss must become a serialized success or a LOUD failure, never a silent exit 0; the probe gets promoted into `tests/` as an N-way regression at a concurrency that currently fails.

**Task 238 premise CORRECTED** (old rationale preserved in place per the decision-trail rule). Its data-loss half became 239; only the speculative *semantic*-collision half remains, with a narrowed trigger.

**The meta-lesson (the one worth keeping).** D-248 says a deferral needs a *named, checkable* trigger. This adds: **when the trigger's condition is cheaply testable, TEST IT rather than declaring it unfired.** I wrote "no parallel-agent surface today" as though it were an observation; it was an assumption, and a ~20-line probe falsified it in minutes — finding a shipped data-loss bug in the process. The D-267 fired-walk at each minor's sweep should probe cheap conditions, not just re-read them.

---

## 2026-07-20 — D-364: NOTE — ECC (`affaan-m/ecc`) studied at code level: a harness OS, not a memory rival; 5 non-memory borrows; one logged self-correction

**Trigger.** The user asked how the kit stacks up against [`affaan-m/ecc`](https://github.com/affaan-m/ecc) (v2.0.0) before continuing the v0.6.0 lane. Full note: [`docs/research/2026-07-20-ecc-harness-os-comparison.md`](../research/2026-07-20-ecc-harness-os-comparison.md).

**Verdict: different category.** ECC is an agent *harness operating system* — 278 skill dirs, 67 agents, 94 commands, ~12 harnesses, 230 contributors, npm + GitHub App + paid Pro tier + 12 doc languages. Memory is one subsystem inside it and the least-invested one. It competes on breadth where the kit competes on memory depth.

**Their memory, verified in code:** LLM-written markdown session summaries at `~/.claude/session-data/*.tmp` — machine-local, **never committed**, stored in ONE GLOBAL dir (project scoping is retrieval-side matching only), 30-day pruned, ≤8KB re-injected at SessionStart — plus per-project instinct YAML (`ecc-homunculus/projects/<git-remote-hash>/`) whose observations archive is **deleted** at 30 days, never distilled. **Zero of six memory primitives present** (durable fact files / search / citations / dedup / conflict detection / tombstones / evolving trust) — each verified by search, not inferred. Their `state.db` is a control-plane store whose `decisions` table is session-CASCADE-scoped and written by *release* tooling; `ecc2`'s `graph recall` is a substring scan over an `updated_at` window and is **not wired into any hook**. Every retention mechanism they have is time-based expiry: *it forgets on a timer rather than curating*.

**Their documented-vs-shipped miss (the class the D-153 rule targets):** `continuous-learning-v2/SKILL.md` documents confidence increasing/decreasing with observation and user correction. Unimplemented — `instinct-cli.py` reads/filters/sorts/averages `confidence` 55 times and **writes it zero times**; it is set once at creation from raw frequency. `skill_runs` records the exact success/failure signal a trust loop needs and nothing reads it back. Task 194's survival gate is ahead of the field's most-cited "continuous learning" implementation.

**SELF-CORRECTION (decision-trail, kept visible).** The first-pass claim — *"their instinct pipeline is essentially our Task 95 dream re-curation, already shipped"* — was **wrong**, and was decided from their architecture DIAGRAM rather than code: the same docs-claim-vs-code-ships error being audited in them, committed mid-audit. Corrected after the user challenged it (*"Task 95 did not ship, how did you decide that?"*). ECC ships the **generation half** (observe → instinct → cluster → promote, gated at 2+ projects / 0.8 avg confidence); the **re-curation half is absent**. Task 95's hard part is unbuilt there too — which makes 95 + 194 differentiators, not catch-up. Captured as `P-ENJNBQ7N`.

**Borrows (all NON-memory — their strength is operations):** (1) **the stale-replay guard** — `session-start.js:651-671` wraps injected prior-session context in `HISTORICAL REFERENCE ONLY — NOT LIVE INSTRUCTIONS`, added after a real bug (their #1534) where post-compaction the model re-ran an `ARGUMENTS=`-bearing slash command with stale arguments, duplicating issues/branches/tasks. **The kit's exposure is worse:** `AUTHORITATIVE_MEMORY_PREAMBLE` says "injected memory wins / lead with memory" with NO line between durable knowledge and stale work-state, while its snapshots carry `Active Threads` bullets naming already-shipped tasks → candidate v0.6.x rider, `P-RYN7KWJJ`. (2) a **PreCompact** hook (they capture at the compaction boundary; the kit only compresses at SessionEnd). (3) **catalog/registry CI gates** — independent validation of the Task 186 approach. (4) **supply-chain-watch** workflow + SLSA3 provenance (their curated IOC blocklist itself is too high-maintenance to copy). (5) **agent-proximity collision avoidance** — novel prior art for parallel agents on one repo, unrelated to memory.

**Not taken:** their memory design, in any part.

---

## 2026-07-19 — D-363: DECISION — Task 175 `cmk tour` shipped: the onboarding explainer + per-agent slash surfaces; `.claude/commands/` becomes a kit-owned scaffold class

The awrshift `/tour` borrow (D-215) built: `tour.mjs::buildTour` narrates the REAL tiers (counts + actual fact titles; the honesty contract pins every surfaced example to a file on disk, and the empty-install path reuses the indexer's seed-bullet filter so a fresh scaffold honestly reads as "nothing captured yet"). Tour EXPLAINS, doctor CHECKS — kept distinct, each pointing at the other. **Two structural notes:** (1) `.claude/commands/` joins `.claude/skills/` as a KIT-OWNED scaffold dir — same Task-230 refresh + downgrade-guard rules (the /tour command file updates with the kit, never treated as user data); (2) the Kiro slash surface = a `manual`-inclusion steering file (Kiro surfaces those as on-demand slash commands — the closed-products study's own finding put to work), written + husk-clean-removed by the Kiro orchestrator; its frontmatter must carry the full `---` fences (the first cut passed a bare string and the uninstall husk-check couldn't recognize the leftover — caught by the uninstall test). CLI-only by class (like doctor/stats — the in-conversation surface is the slash command running the CLI, not an MCP tool). _Relates D-215 (the borrow), D-343/Task 230 (the kit-owned refresh class the commands dir joins), Task 50 (the per-agent-surface seam shape), D-363._

## 2026-07-19 — D-362: DECISION — ADR-0024 (Task 149): recall stays judgment-pulled, hardened into the composed hybrid (→ Task 233); Task 51 CLOSED as absorbed; the Letta deep-read joins the base

**The 15-system trigger study** ([note](../research/2026-07-19-recall-trigger-architecture-study.md)) + the user-commissioned [Letta deep-read](../research/2026-07-19-letta-deep-read.md) settled the judgment-pulled-vs-always-search fork. **The load-bearing finding: the recall model follows WHO OWNS THE CONVERSATION LOOP** — loop-owners pipeline retrieval (one line, benchmarks reward it), MCP servers are structurally judgment-pulled (a tool description is their only trigger surface), and every system in the kit's exact position (Claude Code plugin with hooks + tools: memsearch, claude-mem, pro-workflow) converges on the kit's hybrid — with the one true per-prompt semantic inject (claude-mem's) shipped DEFAULT-OFF as "experimental". Memora nuance recorded against our earlier note: it validates the LADDER (policy-guided multi-step > one-shot, 0.863 > 0.825 at ~98% fewer tokens), NOT the trigger (its retrieval start is unconditional). Letta's trajectory (archival tools deprecated, pushed surface growing, recall scaffolded by doctrine + tool-strategy + existence advertisement) argues the same hybrid. **Verdict (ADR-0024):** keep judgment-pulled as the deep spine; close the documented under-fire class (D-40/D-153) with three composed borrows → **Task 233** (gated FTS5 pointer hint behind a score floor; the Letta existence-advertisement line in the snapshot; the in-skill Memora refinements), measured by the D-122 fire-rate trend. Per-prompt SEMANTIC injection + inject-everything REJECTED with reasons. **Task 51 CLOSED as absorbed** (Task 126's transcripts scope + 226/227 already deliver session-rollup recall; promoting rollups into the facts scope rejected — duplication, churn, and it would blur the curated-vs-record scope split). _Relates D-153 (the fix this architecture-checks), D-326 (the lane), ADR-0023/D-361 (the sibling verdict), Tasks 232/233 (the two ADR build slices), D-362._

## 2026-07-19 — D-361: DECISION — ADR-0023 (Task 176): graph recall split verdict — ACTIVATE the edges we already write (→ Task 232), DEFER derivation behind a relational-qtype benchmark trigger, REJECT graph-DB/typed-KG/hand-authored shapes

**The 9-system code sweep** ([note](../research/2026-07-19-graph-memory-code-sweep.md)) settled the D-216/D-219 question with three facts: (1) the kit's written edges are INVISIBLE end-to-end — `related:` isn't indexed, isn't in `mk_get`, and its targets aren't FTS-searchable (probed live), so backlinks + supersession chains are genuinely unanswerable AND there's no reinforcement loop for the model to write more edges; (2) of six real relational query shapes over this corpus, hybrid + the agentic ladder answers four — the graph-only remainder is real but low-frequency; (3) the field's graph flagships under-deliver in shipped code (mem0 clone: no graph store; MemOS: relational path commented out; Letta: no graph at all) — the marketing is stronger than the revealed preference. **Verdict (ADR-0023):** the S-sized ACTIVATION slice ships as **Task 232** (edges table rebuilt-from-markdown like FTS, supersession CTE, `cmk links`/`mk_links`, successor-naming labels — zero LLM, iwe-grade rebuild); Memora-cue derivation waits behind a NAMED trigger (add a relational/multi-hop qtype to the Task-99 bench; adopt cues only if flat hybrid measurably under-performs there — the D-360 measure-first shape); graph DBs, typed-KG stores, and hand-authored graphs are rejected with reasons (server class D-64; drift + second-source-of-truth; auto-capture users never hand-build note webs — pulse8's agent-edges-only-in-graph.json is the concrete data-loss cautionary). _Relates D-216/D-219/D-225/D-226/D-227 (the leads this closes), ADR-0002 (the rebuildability constraint that shaped every verdict), Task 66/209 (graphiti's gems, already extracted without the graph), Task 232 (the spawn), D-361._

## 2026-07-19 — D-360: DECISION — Task 178 fusion benchmark: KEEP-CURRENT (the kit already ships the RRF the task proposed adopting); temporal decay REJECTED as the wrong mechanism; title-only embedding REJECTED by numbers

**The characterization beat the benchmark to the headline:** the kit's hybrid merge (`search.mjs::reciprocalRankFusion`, design §9.3) is `weight/(k+rank)` with k=60 and equal 0.5/0.5 weights — ranking-identical to pro-workflow's plain RRF (`embeddings.ts:128`, same formula, same k). "Adopt RRF" was a no-op question; the measurable question was whether any ALTERNATIVE beats it. **Measured on the Task-99 harness (hybrid baseline 0.941 R@5 / 1.000 R@10 / 0.860 NDCG@10):** RRF k=20, k=10, captain-claw's weighted-sum (raw AND min-max-normalized) all TIE exactly — at kit scale the fusion stage is not the recall bottleneck. **Two seductive additions rejected with evidence:** (1) captain-claw's 21-day-half-life decay-on-score collapses paraphrase R@5 0.900→0.000 (it buries old-but-true facts — AGE is the wrong penalty signal; SUPERSESSION is the right one, and Task 66's validity windows + Task 209's labels already implement it); (2) Memora's title-only embedding loses on every metric (0.882/0.941/0.750 vs content 0.941/1.000/0.848 — the calibrated expectation held: rich facts don't fragment, so discarding body signal only loses). **Re-open trigger:** a 10k-scale corpus AND an observed ranking failure attributable to fusion. [Research note](../research/2026-07-19-fusion-benchmark.md); the experiment harness stayed a scratch artifact (research-only; no production change shipped). _Relates D-109/D-72 (the bake-off discipline + the shipped recipe), Task 65/99 (the hybrid + the gate), Task 66 (why decay loses to windows), D-217/D-219/D-221/D-225 (the leads this closes), D-360._

## 2026-07-19 — D-359: DECISION — Task 229: the taxonomy vocabulary mapped in docs; the pitfalls mapping lives in ONE place (README), everything else points at it

Docs-only rider shipped: README gains the 4-row "memory types, in kit terms" table + the pitfalls→mechanism paragraph — the canonical statement, mirrored in compact form on the npm README (the same front door in its two homes; the review noted the two copies can drift, accepted as the standing README↔npm-README pairing every capability bullet already lives with); ARCHITECTURE's layer intro maps layers→types and POINTS at the README table; the glossary gains the four terms as a cluster after [[Type taxonomy]], each entry naming its pitfall answer and explicitly disambiguated from the kit's own type (fact PURPOSE) and shape (fact TIME) taxonomies — the three classification axes were begging to be confused. _Relates D-326 (the sweep that found the gap: "implemented, never named"), Task 227 (the citations the episodic entry cites), D-359._

## 2026-07-19 — D-358: DECISION — Task 227: recall citations completed (date + heading on every hit); the §16.39 `cites` param CLOSED permanently (`links` + provenance cover both halves)

**The date + heading were always in the index (created_at, heading_path) — never surfaced.** Now every search hit carries `date` (facts: from `created_at`; transcript-scope: parsed from the day-file NAME, with undated files like `recent.md` deliberately null — their sections carry their own date headings) + `heading`, alongside id + `source_file:line`. The CLI prints a date column; the memory-search skill's Output section now instructs citing the when + where per recalled item.

**The skill-review round (all fixed in-branch):** Important — the transcripts-scope SEMANTIC path was left without dates, the exact class the facts-scope live-bug taught, half-applied (fixed: `enrichTranscriptDates`, both scopes now share the one-owner-after-the-mode-branch pattern; unit-tested via the exported seam, no embedder needed). Minors — §16.39's "Current behavior" paragraph relabeled as history + the stale candidate-status line struck; CLI.md gained the output-columns bullet (a user-visible output change the validators structurally can't see — the D-249 walk's silent class). **Plus a pre-existing adjacent bug the reviewer found verifying units:** the semantic `--since` filter compared `created_at * 1000` (assumed seconds; the column is epoch MS) so it could never exclude a row — silently ignored since the semantic backend shipped. Fixed ms-to-ms, and the whole semantic post-filter extracted as the pure `semanticRowPassesFilters` seam with its own tests.

**`cites` closed, not wired (revisits §16.39's open trigger with the answer NO):** write-time fact-linking already ships as `links` (→ `related` frontmatter, both surfaces), and recall-time citations are provenance-derived — a hand-supplied `cites` array would duplicate one or the other. The permanent rejection (pointing at `links`) beats removing the schema key (back-compat: empty array stays a no-op) and beats silent-drop (which would tell the model a citation landed when it didn't). Decision trail preserved in the §16.39 closure block; re-open only with evidence neither `links` nor provenance covers (typed knowledge-graph edges, e.g.). _Relates D-326 (the lane), Task 31 (the finding's origin), Task 226 (the ladder this citation polish completes), design §9.3 + §16.39, D-358._

## 2026-07-19 — D-357: NOTE — two field findings: the GHA-username/privacy-mask fixture-collision test class, and the first real cross-project migration (youtube-to-slide → the kit) with the single-project cron-registration limitation it exposed

**1. The fixture-collision class (PR #303's ubuntu-only red):** on GitHub Actions the OS username is literally **`runner`** — a common English word — and the kit's L1 privacy mask replaces local-username occurrences with `«USER»` on every screened write path. A test fixture containing the word "runner" written through `memoryWrite` therefore indexes MASKED on Linux CI and unmasked on a developer box whose username isn't an English word → FTS misses only on CI. The mask worked as designed; the fixture collided. **The rule for future tests:** fixture text that flows through a privacy-screened write path must avoid common-word CI usernames (`runner` above all); when a CI-only red has zero platform-looking code in the diff, check what the ENVIRONMENT changes about the data (usernames, homedirs, locales) before suspecting the code.

**2. The youtube-to-slide migration (the kit's first real cross-project migration, user-ordered):** `cmk install --with-semantic` (refresh over a previously-touched install — Task 230's kit-owned refresh fired correctly) → 7 sessions via `cmk import-sessions` (the v0.6.0 headline's first production use outside a sandbox) → 5 legacy hand-format facts converted through `cmk remember` rich (originals archived at `context/memory/archive/legacy-v1/` — the old frontmatter lacks id/type/title so the kit deliberately skips it; conversion-then-archive is the pattern) → the old CLAUDE.md memory-orchestration sections replaced with a migration note BEFORE `cmk import-claude-md` (trim-first, else the import seeds the retired system's operating manual as facts — 60 proposals shrank to 26 real ones) → the 3 legacy `ytslide-*` scheduled tasks deleted. **The kit limitation it exposed:** `register-crons` uses FIXED task names (`cmk-daily-distill`/`cmk-weekly-curate`) — ONE registered project per machine; a second kit project cannot register scheduled crons and rides the lazy-on-read fallback. Fold into the v0.6.x doctor-UX/upgrade batch (47/48/71 + the D-354 HC-5 work): either per-project task names or an explicit multi-project story, plus doctor language that says WHICH project owns the machine's registration. Also: workspace trust (`hasTrustDialogAccepted`) gates hook writes — a migration isn't done until the user trusts the folder; the assistant cannot set it (the permission classifier hard-blocks `~/.claude.json` edits, correctly — two authorized attempts refused, manual path documented). _Relates D-326/D-355 (import-sessions), Task 230 (the refresh), D-311/D-354 (cron lineage), the 47/48/71 batch, D-357._

## 2026-07-18 — D-356: DECISION — Task 226 build choices: a follow-up verb (`cmk expand`/`mk_expand`), NOT an `--expand` search flag; file-adjacent vs time-adjacent named as two axes; T:-id expansion is head-anchored by construction

**The spec offered two shapes** ("an `--expand` option on search OR an `mk_expand <hit-id>` follow-up"). **Chosen: the follow-up verb.** Why: the ladder's flow is search → pick the ONE hit that looks right → expand THAT — a search-level flag would expand every result (N× the tokens for hits the model is about to discard) and couples the bound to the search call. The verb also lands the natural CLI/MCP parity pair (`expand` ↔ `mk_expand`, the 11th parity op / 12th MCP tool, Kiro-auto-approved via the shared `MCP_AUTO_APPROVE` catalog).

**Two contracts worth the trail:** (1) **expand vs timeline = two AXES, both kept** — expand is file-adjacent (the enclosing heading section in the SOURCE file), timeline is time-adjacent (created_at neighbors); the skill's ladder names expand Step 2 and timeline Step 2b rather than replacing one with the other. (2) **A transcript-chunk id expands head-anchored** — `chunkTranscript` stamps every window of a section with the section HEADING's line, so `T:<file>:<line>` cannot point at the matched text's own line; the bounded window therefore grows from the section head (documented in §9.4 + pinned by the budget-pair test; the test originally assumed text-centered windowing and was corrected to the real contract — a test-was-wrong instance, said explicitly).

**BUG→FIX (the skill-review's Blocking, live-proven by the reviewer, fixed in-branch):** the first build's safety story — tier-aware traversal guard + "unscreened surfaces are never indexed so no hit id points into them" — was FALSE for the T: branch: a T: id is free-form model-suppliable input parsed straight from the string, so `mk_expand` could read any in-project file by path — including `context/.locks/redactions.log` (the plaintext ORIGINAL of every secret the guard ever redacted — the worst possible target), the raw `imported/` floor, `*.live.md`, and `now.md`. The description-vs-enforcement gap is the exact class CLAUDE.md's "prose rules vs enforcement" names, inside a security boundary. **Fix:** the T: branch now gates on the transcript-chunk INDEX (`SELECT 1 FROM transcript_chunks WHERE source_file = ?`) — legit hit ids always pass (they are built FROM that table), the excluded surfaces never do; regression-locked with planted-secret fixtures asserting the refusal AND that no planted content appears in any output. Also fixed from the same pass: the four stale "11 tools" mentions in live docs/comments, and the single-oversized-line cap clamp. _Relates D-326 (the lane), ADR-0014 (shared read core), Task 126 (the transcript-chunk scope + exclusion set), design §9.4, the memory-search skill, D-356._

## 2026-07-18 — D-355: DECISION — Task 225 build choices: committed-LEDGER resumability (not day-file markers alone, not the video's sentinel) · gitignored+un-indexed raw floor · judge-in-the-prompt privacy posture · 50/1200 bounds as the curate-squash mitigation

**The v0.6.0 headline built per its tasks.md spec; four choices made at build time, each with a rejected alternative:**

1. **Resume point = the committed `context/sessions/imported-sessions.md` ledger** (single-writer, append-only, one line per processed session: `imported`/`screened`/`skipped-empty`), with day-file `imported-session:` markers as the secondary guard. **Why not markers alone:** weekly-curate ROTATES >7-day day files into `archive.md` via Haiku summarization — markers die there, so marker-only idempotency breaks a week after import (a re-run would re-buy + duplicate everything). The ledger survives rotation AND `git clone`. Sentinel-run-once (the origin creator's shape) was already rejected in the task spec. A failed backend call is deliberately NOT ledgered — the next run retries exactly the remainder (ADR-0020).
2. **Raw extracts → `context/transcripts/imported/` — gitignored AND excluded from the transcript index.** They are UN-screened conversation text: committing them would violate ADR-0019's fail-closed posture, and indexing them would violate the 148.3 "unscreened content never becomes searchable" invariant. The subdirectory placement keeps them out of `syncTranscriptChunks`'s flat readdir scan structurally. ADR-0010 floor: preserved indefinitely, drillable by the recall ladder.
3. **Privacy = the ADR-0019 sessions-tier posture** (privacy instruction in the SAME summarize call + L1 `maskPii` + `screenBeforeCommittedWrite` scope `all`) — no second L3 judge call per session. Precedent: ADR-0019's consequences line for `today-*.md` ("no extra Haiku call"); the extract filter already drops tool output (the PII incident vector).
4. **`DEFAULT_MAX_SESSIONS=50` (newest-first) + `SUMMARY_MAX_BYTES=1200`** — bounds both the run's spend and the **curate-squash composition edge found during doc-reading:** weekly-curate folds ALL old day files through ONE 4096-byte compress then deletes them, so a months-long bulk import gets one coarse archive fold instead of 26 weekly ones. Mitigated (bounds + raw floor), not fixed; the clean fix — per-week-resumable curate (ADR-0020 applied to curate, which is still all-or-nothing) — is recorded in design §22.4 with a named ship trigger.

**Two lock-tests moved during the build (test-was-wrong class, said explicitly per the anti-pattern rule):** the scaffold suite's no-stub-leaves lock got `import-sessions` added to NON_STUB_VERBS (it is real; unguarded from the repo cwd it would discover the maintainer's REAL history — the exit-2-without---yes default proved itself in that very test run), and the gitignore-fragment "no task numbers" lock caught my Task-225 reference in a user-facing comment (reworded). Also: the install-offer backend inject is `importBackend`, NOT `backend` — `runInstall`'s `options.backend` is the Task-201 `--backend <agent>` override flag (collision caught by the test). _Relates D-326 (the lane), Task 38b/216/148/213 (the reused machinery), ADR-0010/0019/0020, design §22, tasks.md 225._

## 2026-07-18 — D-354: BUG — the v0.5.4 rename silently killed the nightly distill: the schtask's ABSOLUTE path still pointed at the dead `@lh8ppl/claude-memory-kit` package; HC-5 (sentinel-presence) couldn't see it, HC-10 (heartbeat) could

**Found by the user's question ("has the 23:00 distill run at all?"):** `schtasks` showed `Last Result: 1` with the task still targeting `...npm\node_modules\@lh8ppl\claude-memory-kit\bin\cmk-daily-distill.mjs` — the OLD package name. The last successful distill log is **2026-07-14**, the night before the v0.5.4 rename published; every night since failed silently. The D-83 decision to register crons with ABSOLUTE paths (correct for PATH-robustness) has this inherent cost: a package rename/move strands the registration. **The kit's own detection split cleanly:** HC-10 (compaction liveness, outcome-based) correctly FAILED ("looks dead, last ran ~3d ago"); HC-5 (cron registered) PASSED — it checks only the `cron-registered` sentinel, not whether the registered target EXISTS. The false-green half is the same OUTCOME-vs-heartbeat class as D-298/HC-10's own origin story, one check over. **Fix now:** the user re-runs `cmk register-crons` (new path); tonight's run doubles as Task 215's owed popup live-verify. **Durable fix (folded into the D-353 v0.6.x doctor-UX batch, 47/48/71):** HC-5 gains a cron-TARGET-exists check (read the registered command via `schtasks /query` / crontab / launchctl, stat the script path, FAIL with `cmk register-crons` as recovery when the target is gone) — and the upgrade/rename docs gain a "re-run register-crons after a package rename/major upgrade" note. _Relates D-83 (absolute-path registration), D-343 (the rename's other stale-artifact bug — same class: update-in-place installs keep stale registrations), D-298 (outcome-vs-heartbeat), HC-5/HC-10, Task 215 (its live-verify now re-armed), the D-353 doctor batch._

## 2026-07-18 — D-353: DECISION — the user-requested full-backlog quality walk: 27 of 50 open tasks needed action (11 fired-unnoticed triggers, 8 stale dispositions, 8 shipped-but-open); every one re-verdicted

**Why it ran (and what it proves about the structural guard):** the user asked "go over all the tasks — are they all laned or trigger-based?" `validate-backlog-triggers` was GREEN (presence-only, by design — D-256 said trigger QUALITY stays the sweep's judgment) while a third of the backlog had rotted in quality: triggers had FIRED with nobody noticing (the exact D-267 failure: "a named trigger only fires if someone notices"), lanes pointed at shipped versions, and absorbed tasks sat open after their absorbers shipped. A dedicated audit pass read all 50 open entries.

**Closed (8) — deliverable already shipped:** 59 (→66, v0.4.4) · 68 (→95's design, folded at D-352 with its scratchpad-pruning facet added to 95's scope) · 97 (→151.T `trust_score`, live since 194) · 130 (→96's `purge --hard`, v0.5.5) · 147 (completion condition = 156, shipped v0.3.3) · 179 (→ADR-0017, built as 190–194) · 188 (→191) · **224 (the SonarCloud crash — solved same-day by D-350)**.

**Fired-trigger verdicts (11):** 47+48+71 → v0.6.x doctor-UX rider batch (the Task-210 doctor touch fired them; per-touch re-firing is noise — laned instead) · 51+149+176+178 → scheduled into v0.6.0 (it IS the recall-surface minor the trio trigger named) · 73 → v0.6.x upgrade-hygiene rider (Task 230 fired its install-surface trigger; NOT subsumed — 230 refreshes kit-owned scaffold, 73 re-renders user-tier seeds) · 186 → DECIDED: build, v0.6.0 rider (its "decide in the 214 batch" point passed at v0.5.1 undecided) · 174 → auto-flagged KILL-OR-COMMIT at the v0.6.0 cut (fired TWICE unnoticed — the D-248 rot definition).

**Stale refreshes (7):** 55 → remainder rescoped into 95's insight-surfacing · 70 → split (70.5 rides 95 per D-352; the rest triggers on an injection incident or the next security-depth batch) · 80 → trigger: stats/recall-log evidence of env-fact recall misses · 98 → trigger: next scaffold flake · 146 → trigger: a multi-agent/swarm consumer appears · 166 → trigger: kiro-cli V3 GA or a live guardrail-gap report · 196 → rescoped to the on-demand agent tail (core shipped v0.4.5/v0.4.6), trigger: demand.

**Held open deliberately:** 215 (owed the user's live confirmation of a popup-free 23:00 run — asked, not yet answered) · 184/189/217/223 → re-verdict at the v0.6.0 cut per their existing dispositions. **Meta:** the presence validator can't see firing — the D-267 fired-walk has now needed a HUMAN prompt twice ("this sweep starts to sound more like a light wipe" was the first). If a third manual walk finds fired-unnoticed triggers again, graduate the fired-check structurally: teach `validate-backlog-triggers` a machine-checkable trigger vocabulary ("when Task N ships" → checkable against the checkbox) and fail on fired-but-unverdicted. _Relates D-248/D-256/D-267 (the trigger discipline lineage), D-349/D-350/D-352 (the same-day decisions several verdicts consume), the audit output (agent pass over all 50 entries)._

## 2026-07-18 — D-352: DECISION — Task 95's design forks are SETTLED (the D-349 grill, run after the 11-source research review): op-class split · extend-216 screen + source-trust tags · facts-then-self-audit in ONE lane · scheduled against the roll

**The review that preceded it:** the user's paper ("Language Models Need Sleep", arXiv 2606.03979 — [note](../research/2026-07-18-sleep-paradigm-memory-consolidation.md)) + a full re-extraction of all ten prior 95-relevant sources ([synthesis](../research/2026-07-18-task-95-design-input-synthesis.md)). The research settled the op set (ADD/UPDATE/SUPERSEDE-never-DELETE), the shape (deterministic floor → one batched LLM pass → code decides, event-time wins), conservative merging (two independent over-consolidation warnings), read-RAW, the Sleep ordering invariants, and the governance obligations (provenance, deletion-propagation as a regression gate, never-silent-skip). The grill then decided the four genuinely-open questions:

1. **F1 — op-class split.** Safe, reversible, non-destructive ops (exact-dup cleanup, event-time supersede-marks with both sides retained) AUTO-apply under a screened/bounded/audited envelope (the ADR-0017 feedback-screen template); lossy/generative ops (merged wording, new insight entries, anything touching a high-trust fact) land as a reviewable adopt-or-discard diff (the Dreams contract; composes with D-126/Task 150's propose-and-approve).
2. **F2 — extend the SHARED screen, not a dedicated one.** The user challenged whether this was a less-work pick; the answer is on the merits: a second screen for the same channel class recreates the D-337 drift shape (two screens, one catalog gap); Task 216 was built for LLM side-door channels, and extending it (input + output + the Task-70.5 source-trust tags: user-said vs tool-output vs pasted-web) upgrades every channel at once. The "dedicated = more thorough" framing in the grill option was WRONG and is corrected here.
3. **Scope — facts AND the self-audit, one lane, sequenced.** The user's push ("if the only reason is effort, why wait?") surfaced the honest reason: sequencing, not avoidance — the AutoMem-style schema/process self-audit grants the pass authority over the system's own structure and therefore builds SECOND, on the fact-pass's proven envelope (screen → gate → regression check). Both ship in Task 95's lane; the self-audit is not a someday-task.
4. **Cadence — scheduled against the today→archive roll** (the Sleep invariant: consolidate BEFORE the fast tier loses data), riding the existing idle/cron machinery.

**Lane:** unchanged from D-349 — assigned at the next sweep (v0.6.0 is committed to Day-One Memory; 95 lanes after). The needs-design gate is now CLEARED; what remains before build is ordinary task-level design (the mechanism doc + done-criteria), not a fork. _Relates D-349 (the trigger this resolves), D-126/150 (adopt UX), D-337 (the drift precedent behind F2), ADR-0017 (the envelope), Tasks 210/212/213/216/70.5 (the organs the pass composes with)._

## 2026-07-18 — D-351: BUG+FIX — a time-bomb test turned main red four days after it was written: the CLI stats surface had no injected-`now` seam, so fixed-date fixtures aged out of the real-clock window

**The bomb:** `cli-memory-stats.test.js` seeds log entries relative to a hardcoded `NOW = '2026-07-14'`. Every `computeMemoryStats` test injects `now: NOW` — but the CLI-surface test calls `runStatsMemoryHealth`, which had **no `now` seam** (it used the real clock). The seeded entries aged out of the real 7-day window as calendar days passed: green July 14–17, red on the 18th — firing on an unrelated docs push (the D-350 memory flush), which briefly looked like the culprit. **Fix:** `runStatsMemoryHealth` gains `deps.now` (forwarded to `computeMemoryStats` — completing the Task-113 injected-deps pattern the test's own name claims), and the test injects `NOW` like its siblings. **The class:** a test whose fixture dates are FIXED but whose code-path clock is REAL is a delayed-action failure that lands on whoever pushes next — same family as the validate-test-ids/budget-pair classes: if the pattern recurs, a `validate-*` check ("a test file with a hardcoded ISO-date constant must not call a CLI surface lacking a now-injection") is the structural graduation; one instance stays a lesson. _Relates Task 212 (the stats surface), Task 113 (the injected-deps pattern), D-350 (the push it fired on)._

## 2026-07-18 — D-350: BUG+FIX — D-341 OVERTURNED: the SonarCloud exit-3 crash was triggered by OUR OWN test fixtures all along; fixed by making projectRoot fixtures runtime temp dirs (first green scan since 2026-07-11)

**The correction (the user's "maybe they fixed it? check for updates" prompt is what re-opened it):** D-341 concluded "a server-side SonarSource regression, NOT reachable from this repo" after disproving six config levers — the A3S feature-flag rollout timestamp (2026-07-11 12:42 UTC) matched the crash start exactly. But that minute ALSO matched **PR #278's merge**, which added `projectRoot: 'C:\proj'` fixtures to `cli-register-crons.test.js`. **The original investigation grepped only the forward-slash form `C:/proj` and missed the escaped-backslash fixtures** — the same-timestamp flag rollout then looked like the only correlate. (The verification lesson: when grepping for a Windows path, grep BOTH separator forms — a forward-slash-only grep on a `\\`-escaped fixture is a false negative that misdirected a whole investigation.)

**The proven mechanism (three experiments on PR #299):** SonarCloud's A3S context collector **partial-evaluates** `join(projectRoot, 'context', …)` in the analyzed source, **chasing caller argument values into analysis-EXCLUDED test files** (which is why excluding `tests/**` never helped — the source file is legitimately analyzed; the collector chases its callers), derives the phantom path, and `opendir`'s it on the Linux runner — crashing the whole scan on ENOENT instead of skipping. Evidence chain: (1) the crash path **followed a fixture rename** (`proj→sandbox`); (2) it **folded straight through a `map+join` obfuscator** (`sandbox→sbx-root`) — the engine fully partial-evaluates string construction; (3) it **disappeared** when the fixtures became a runtime `mkdtempSync` dir the engine cannot statically know. Also verified en route: SonarJS 13.3.0 (released 2026-07-17, server-delivered same day) does NOT fix it — same signature.

**The fix:** projectRoot fixtures that flow into dir-joins are now RUNTIME temp dirs (statically unknowable, deterministic assertions unchanged — they never referenced the root literal). String obfuscation alone is insufficient (folded). **Vendor residue (report-worthy, non-blocking):** crash-not-skip on a phantom ENOENT + reading analysis-excluded files; the `enable-a3s-context-collection` flag was the necessary condition, our fixture the sufficient trigger — both the D-341 flag-correlation and this fixture-correlation were true; only the "unreachable from the repo" inference was wrong. **Meta:** the wrong conclusion survived three sessions because it ANSWERED the question with a subset of the truth (the flag correlation was real) — the same class as the convention-convergence rule; the fix came from re-examining the raw evidence (the opendir path's exact shape) instead of re-reading the recorded conclusion. _Relates D-341 (overturned on reachability, preserved per decision-trail), D-324, Task 224, PR #278 (the trigger), PR #299 (the experiments + fix)._

## 2026-07-17 — D-349: DECISION — v0.5.5 backlog sweep: Task 95 (dream re-curation) stays DEFERRED with a named grill-trigger; v0.5.5 cuts on the four shipped governance tasks

**The minor-cut backlog sweep (D-248 forcing function) before tagging v0.5.5.** The lane was **Task 96 + 210 + 230 + 231 + Task 95 *if its needs-design gate cleared*** — the first four shipped (PRs #293/#294/#295/#296), Task 95 was the open question. **Trigger-fired walk (D-267):** Task 95's SEQUENCING gates have all become true since the last sweep — Task 66 (temporal validity) shipped v0.4.4, the learn-loop Phase 1–2 (Tasks 190–194) shipped through v0.5.3, and the Task-179 umbrella closed into ADR-0017 (95 consumes the loop's utility data). So the "after 66 + Phase 1" trigger FIRED. **But firing the sequencing trigger ≠ ready to build:** the task carries TWO unresolved DESIGN forks, each of which materially changes the engine — (F1) review-gate-vs-auto-apply (the Dreams "input never modified, reviewable output" model vs auto-apply-with-revert, and how it composes with SETTLED D-126 no-auto-commit) and (F2) raw-transcript privacy (re-curating from RAW transcripts per the D-44 classify-over-raw lesson feeds unscreened verbatim to the curation LLM — a NEW unscreened READ channel that Task 231's screen-then-mask, a WRITE-path fix, doesn't cover). **Verdict: keep deferred, refresh the trigger to a checkable one** — "both forks decided in a grill session." This is the correct autopilot call: a large design-heavy engine (absorbs F-D + 55/66/84 + curate) with genuine open forks is exactly the "don't let the agent build the wrong thing" case the grilling skill exists for; building it blind would fabricate a design the user never ratified. The autopilot proceeds by giving 95 a named trigger (not "when ready") and cutting v0.5.5 on the four shipped tasks — which is what the lane's conditional wording anticipated. _Relates D-248 (named-trigger + minor-sweep forcing function), D-267 (trigger-fired walk), D-126 (no-auto-commit, the F1 constraint), D-44 (classify-over-raw, the F2 driver), ADR-0017 (the umbrella that cleared the sequencing gate), Task 66/190–194 (the shipped dependencies)._

## 2026-07-16 — D-348: FIX — Task 210 ships the deletion-propagation guarantee (HC-12 + the daily-distill forward filter): a forgotten fact is VERIFIABLY gone from the derived surfaces, not just tombstoned

**The gap (Always-On survey, arXiv 2606.30306 — the field's least-implemented invariant, rollback 27/435):** `forget()` tombstones the fact + reindexes the FTS index (Task 110), but nothing verified the retraction reached the DISTILLED summaries — a `recent.md`/`archive.md`/`today-*.md` summary distilled BEFORE the forget still repeats the deleted content. "Deletion is a cascade through the derivation graph, not a single operation."

**Built (`deletion-propagation.mjs`, two exports):** `checkDeletionPropagation` (the report-first check — for each tombstoned fact, verify absence from the SQLite index AND the summaries, matched on distinctive content + citation id; returns `{checked, vacuous, survivals:[{id,surface,path}]}`) and `screenTombstonedContent` (the FORWARD filter — drops already-tombstoned lines from a fresh distilled day before it's banked). Wired as **HC-12** in `cmk doctor` (FAIL names the survivor's exact file+id, recovery = reindex for an index survival / redact-or-re-distill for a summary; SKIP is explicitly *vacuous*) and into `daily-distill`'s banking site (`tombstone_dropped` counted in the NDJSON log, never silent).

**The AOEP two-sided discipline (the survey's method, adopted as the test shape):** every check has an OBLIGATION side (a forgotten fact IS absent) AND a **negative-invariant** side (an empty history is LABELED vacuous — a no-deletion system must never read as "verified"; the test asserts `vacuous:true` on an empty dir, and the obligation tests assert the fixture SURFACED the fact FIRST so a check that saw nothing can't pass silently). This is a candidate `validate-*` shape if it recurs, per Prose-vs-enforcement.

**Scope, deliberate:** report-first on the check side (scrubbing a distilled summary composes with Task 96's `cmk redact` — the check routes there rather than auto-editing prose); P-tier tombstones only (`consolidate()`'s hard-dropped L/M bullets leave no tombstone — that's lifecycle-map G2 / Task 91.2, a distinct write-side gap, not this check's blind spot). The forward filter closes the window going forward; the check surfaces the legacy survivals a scrub still owes.

**Review round (1 Blocking + 4 Important + 2 Minor, all fixed in-branch) — the forward filter's granularity was wrong.** Self-review first caught a window (a forget AFTER a day was banked left content in the artifact → added an assembly-time re-screen). Then the skill pass found the **Blocking**: the forward filter DROPPED WHOLE LINES on a needle hit — but a distilled bullet routinely consolidates several facts, so dropping the line took live facts + their citations with it (silent data loss from `recent.md`). **PIVOT: whole-line drop → SPAN-replace with `[deleted]`** (mirroring Task 96 redact's scrub-the-span-keep-the-record posture) — the tombstoned span goes, the surrounding live content survives. Plus four Important: the assembly re-screen dropped silently (now counted in `tombstone_dropped`); a `[redacted: …]` marker became a false needle that would drop every co-scrubbed fact (now stripped before needle extraction); the forward filter was blind past the bound with no signal (now `tombstone_screen_truncated` in the log); and HC-12 flagged raw `today-*.md` sources that no scrub can reach (now scans only the DERIVED `today-*.distilled.md`, not the raw buffer). Minors: surface-aware `recoveryCommand` (reindex is a no-op on a summary survival), and read-tombstones-once (M2). Tests 11 → 16.

**Gates:** 3119 green; stress 5/5; HC-12 **live-verified** with the repo binary (SKIP-vacuous → FAIL-naming-the-survivor); new lifecycle-map edge #26 + G2-cousin note; HC-12 in HEALTH-CHECKS + CLI.md + design §9.2.1. _Relates the Always-On survey, Task 96 (the scrub the check routes to + the marker-boilerplate class), Task 110 (forget's index cascade — the first half), memory-lifecycle-map edge #26 + G2, D-308._

## 2026-07-16 — D-347: BUG+FIX — Task 96's scrub surface was narrower than the kit's dual-write graph (live-test + skill-review round); redact/purge now cover filename, L-tier pads, DECISIONS.md, and JSON-aware audit echoes

**The live-test finding (the D-84 class, again):** unit-green redact left `cmk search "hunter2"` returning a hit — the fact **FILENAME** is a slug of the title, so a title-borne secret leaked into the committed *path* (INDEX links, search `source_file`, audit `paths.after`) after a clean body scrub. Fix: rename the live fact file to the scrubbed title's slug when the basename is actually contaminated (slug-form containment, or writeFact-canonical-of-leaked-title for cap-truncated secrets; `renameSkipped` surfaced when no clean name exists); archive copies stay id-named (resolveFact contract).

**The skill-review round (3 Blocking + 4 Important + 7 Minor, ONE shape):** *the scrub's surface enumeration was narrower than the kit's actual dual-write graph.* (B1) a hand-rolled scratchpad list missed every L-tier pad — including `context.local/private.md`, exactly where the sensitivity screen routes secret-adjacent facts → now derived from `SCRATCHPADS_BY_TIER` (the shared-modules rule, again). (B2) the committed `DECISIONS.md` journal dual-writes every project fact's title+Why and was neither scrubbed nor reported → redact span-scrubs THIS fact's marker-bounded entry (append-only forbids deletion, not redaction — the retract precedent); purge removes the entry outright; `remainingElsewhere` scans the journal. (B3) purge re-echoed the secret-slug path into audit.log → purge records NO path and scrubs the removed filenames from historical entries. (I4) the advisory printed `--path "context/" --invert-paths` — which erases the whole memory tier's history → mode-split: `--replace-text` (span) after redact, path-scoped invert on exactly the purged file(s) after purge; U/L tiers (not committed) get a rotate-anyway note instead. (I5/I6) the audit scrub was byte-literal — missing `"`/`\`-bearing patterns (escaped on disk) and racing lock-free appenders → JSON-aware per-line parse→replace→re-stringify with a bounded re-apply. (I7) an edited scratchpad was excluded wholesale from the remaining-count, silently hiding stray non-bullet lines → only the fact file is excluded.

**Meta:** the two-pass discipline earned its keep again — self-review caught the purge path echo; skill-review caught the four surfaces self-review's mental model hid (it was anchored on "the fact file + its bullet", not the write graph). Tests 22 → 30, incl. an L-tier pad pin, journal scrub/removal pins, an escaped-pattern audit pin, and a purge over-mutation pin. _Relates D-346, ADR-0022, Task 210 (its propagation checker now has MORE expected post-states to treat as clean), D-84 (unit-green ≠ works-on-real-input)._

## 2026-07-16 — D-346: FIX — Task 96 ships the compliance scrub: `cmk redact` (app-layer complete, ADR-0022) + `cmk purge --hard` (the one sanctioned hard-delete); the recovery half stays a git recipe, not a feature

**Built exactly to the ratified ADR-0022:** `redact.mjs` exposes `redactFact` (scrub a literal pattern — never a regex, ReDoS/mis-scope posture — from the live fact OR its tombstoned/superseded copy, the dual-written scratchpad bullet in place, plus in-band INDEX/FTS reindex per the forget/D-85 no-follow-up pattern; the fact + a secret-free audit entry survive, pattern identified by LENGTH only) and `purgeHard` (live + every archive copy + bullet lines + index rows gone, NO tombstone, double-flagged `--hard --yes`). Both CLI-only — the never-MCP §6.5 contract is PINNED by a test that greps mcp-server.mjs for `mk_redact|mk_purge`. The `gitAdvisory` prints every time: rotate-first + the `filter-repo` one-time team operation (SECURITY.md runbook added) — the kit never rewrites history. Per-fact scope is explicit: `remainingElsewhere` counts (never scrubs) the same pattern in other facts. The v0.1.x `purge` stub is finally real.

**The bug the task's own TDD caught before it ever shipped:** the redaction marker `[redacted: reason date]` contains `[` + `:` — a raw byte-replace inside an unquoted YAML `title:` produced INVALID frontmatter and made the fact unresolvable (the idempotency test failed with `not-found` on the second run — the file had effectively vanished from the kit's view). Fix: frontmatter-AWARE replacement (parse → replace across string fields + body → re-serialize via frontmatter.mjs/js-yaml, which quotes the marker properly), raw-replace only as the fallback for an already-unparseable file (better scrubbed-but-odd than leaked). The scrub verb that corrupts what it scrubs is exactly the class this repo's own-tests-first discipline exists to catch.

**The recovery half (the reframe honored):** no `cmk recover` wrapper shipped — git already gives point-in-time (`git show <sha>:context/...`) and ADR-0022 item 4 scoped anything more OUT. Recorded here so the decision isn't re-derived: the "recovery" deliverable of the original Task-96 framing was ~80% git all along.

**Verified:** TDD (16 tests — live/tombstone/superseded scrub, bullet-in-place, index+search post-scrub, secret-free audit, over-mutation, remaining-elsewhere, idempotency, purge paths, the never-MCP pin, real-binary advisory + exit codes). _Relates ADR-0022 (ratified by the user this session), D-62/D-218 (the reframe), §6.5, Task 210 (composes — the propagation checker treats `[redacted: …]` as expected post-state), Task 96, D-346._

## 2026-07-16 — D-345: FIX — Task 230 ships the install scaffold-refresh: kit-owned files update on re-install, user memory never does; HC-9 sees content drift (closes the D-343 installer half)

**The user's requirement, executed verbatim:** *"if I change anything in the next versions and re-install on a project, I want it to update to the current version — the only thing it should not overwrite is the memory."* `installTier` gains an `overwrite` mode; the `.claude/skills/` call site passes it (kit-owned code — kit updates win, a hand-edit is deliberately overwritten); the user-data tiers (`context/`, `context.local/`, user tier) keep skip-if-exists as their CONTRACT (an edited MEMORY.md/USER.md/fact file is never clobbered — pinned by an over-mutation test). Refresh is idempotent (byte-identical → skip) and never silent: the result gains a `refreshed` array and the human install line names the count (found live: the old line said "your edits preserved" while refreshing — now "your memory preserved, N kit file(s) refreshed").

**Detection half:** HC-9 now content-compares the kit-owned scaffold against the installed binary's template (`kitOwnedScaffoldDrift`, exported from install.mjs) and FAILs naming the drifted file(s) — the marker only proves `cmk install` ran at this version, not that every kit-owned file matches (the exact D-343 false-green: marker v0.5.4, skill still pre-rename). A missing skill is NOT drift (a `skipClaudeFiles` Kiro/Cursor project legitimately has none).

**Contract pivot recorded (decision-trail):** `cli-skill-scaffold.test.js`'s "re-install preserves a hand-edited skill" test pinned the ORIGINAL Task-69 contract — it was asserting exactly the D-343 bug, so it changed WITH the contract (now asserts refresh + the `refreshed` report; justification in the test comment; the memory-tier over-mutation guard lives in cli-install-refresh.test.js). The install.mjs call-site comment preserves the original rationale + the pivot.

**Verified:** TDD (2 red on exactly the new behaviors → green), suite 3070/3070, live-tested with the REPO binary (staleify → HC-9 FAIL naming the file → reinstall → refreshed to 0 stale refs → HC-9 PASS; MEMORY.md byte-identical across the reinstall) — the first live run accidentally used the GLOBAL cmk and "reproduced the bug", the exact old-version trap the live-test rule names. Stress: skipped with reason (pure file-mutation + in-process doctor check, no spawn/hook/concurrency surface — the Task-214/220 class).

**Skill-review pass (1 Blocking + 2 Important, all fixed in-branch):** (1) **Blocking — the benign-downgrade contract was broken twice:** `checkVersionDrift` deliberately PASSes when the project scaffold is NEWER than the binary (an older global cli elsewhere — not drift, D-176), but the new drift check ran on ANY pass and the skills overwrite had no version guard — so machine-B's older cli would false-FAIL HC-9 with a factually wrong message whose own recovery (`cmk install`) silently DOWNGRADED the skills, while the CLAUDE.md block correctly refused the same downgrade. Fixed: the drift check gates on EXACT marker==binary version; the skills overwrite gates on not-a-downgrade (`--force` opts in, mirroring the block rule); both pinned by tests. (2) **Important — CRLF false-drift flap (the kit's own D-126 class):** both compares were exact-byte and the gitattributes fragment didn't cover `.claude/skills/` — a Windows autocrlf checkout of an identical skill would fail HC-9 forever and be rewritten every install against every checkout. Fixed: EOL-normalized compares (CRLF-only diff = identical, no rewrite) + `.claude/skills/**/*.md text eol=lf` in the fragment; pinned. (3) **Important — doc-walk miss:** `docs/CLAUDE-CODE.md` (a source-of-truth-table row) still said "re-running skips existing files" — updated to the new contract. Minors: a structural no-`{{`-placeholders-in-skill-templates test (the vars-parity flap the comment only claimed to pin), the install() header contract documents `refreshed` + the overwrite-requires-refreshed rule, drift paths normalized to `/` in the HC-9 message, and a pre-existing unescaped-pipe table-rendering bug in CLAUDE-CODE.md fixed in passing. _Relates D-343 (the finding), D-176 (the benign-downgrade contract honored), D-126 (the CRLF class), Task 230, the decision-trail rule (the Task-69 skip contract preserved + pivoted), D-345._

## 2026-07-15 — D-344: FIX — Task 231 ships screen-then-mask: the D-337 invisible-unicode bypass is closed on both write paths, and rich-path errors exit 2 (closes D-338)

**The fix (D-337 option (a), executed as decided — no re-litigation):** both shared write boundaries now run `checkPoisonGuard` on the privacy-stripped, PRE-mask text — the pipeline is `sanitizePrivacyTags` → `checkPoisonGuard` → `maskPii` in `memoryWrite` (the scratchpad add path) AND `writeFact` (the rich-fact path, title+body). The guard stays after the `<private>` strip (the C5 contract — user-marked-private content is removed by request, never rejected) and moves before the mask (maskPii strips invisible/zero-width/bidi codepoints — running it first destroyed the guard's evidence, the D-337 composition bug). Behavior change is exactly the intended one: invisible/bidi text now REJECTS (exit 2, nothing written, `***`-redacted Door-5 log) instead of silently-strips-and-writes; ordinary PII (emails/home-paths/usernames) still masks and writes.

**The exit-code half (one line, closes D-338):** `runRememberRich` now sets `process.exitCode = 2` on every writeFact error — a rich-path poison rejection, an invalid `--shape` (the D-338 finding), and a title collision were all printed-but-exit-0, invisible to scripts and to the PR5 cut-gate probe. Collision exit 2 decided + pinned (error class = non-zero, consistent with the bare path).

**Why the suite never caught it (recorded for the class):** `cli-poison-guard.test.js` tested the guard FUNCTION in isolation — correct at every version. The bug was the ORDER two separately-correct layers composed in (§6.7 stated "guard before write", §6.10 stated "mask before hash/dedup/disk"; neither owned the guard↔mask cross-invariant — the design docs had the same gap as the code). New coverage: `tests/cli-screen-then-mask.test.js` (11 tests) drives the REAL `cmk` binary (bare + rich + `--json` stdin channel, the exact PR5 probe shape) AND both module boundaries in-process, with the C5 `<private>` contract + mask-still-applies controls and an over-mutation guard. Design §6.7 + §6.10 now both state the sanitize→screen→mask contract explicitly.

**Verified:** TDD (8 red for the right reasons → green), full suite 3061/3061, stress 5/5 first invocation, live-tested on the real repo binary in a sandbox (bare U+200B → exit 2 `injection_invisible_unicode`; rich U+200B → exit 2; clean email write → exit 0, masked, nothing leaked; rejected probes wrote nothing).

**Skill-review pass (two Important findings, both fixed in-branch before merge — the two-pass discipline earning its keep on a security PR):**
1. **Catalog drift — the bypass survived for U+2062/63/64.** The mask's invisible-codepoint list had the invisible math operators; the guard's didn't — so even after the reorder, those three codepoints were silently stripped-and-written (the reviewer proved it live). Root fix, not a patch: `INVISIBLE_UNICODE_CODEPOINTS` is now ONE exported catalog in poison-guard.mjs (the union, 20 codepoints, frozen); pii-patterns derives its strip-set from it. The same no-owner-of-the-cross-invariant shape as D-337 itself, one level down. Test: U+2063 through the real binary → exit 2.
2. **Blast-radius correction (the docs claimed too much):** the guard now sees PRE-sanitized paths, so `password: /home/<20+ chars>` matches `secret_generic_credential` and REJECTS where it previously slipped through `~`-sanitized (reviewer-proven). **Decision: ACCEPT the sharpened guard** — a security screen erring conservative on keyword-adjacent paths is correct; the old pass-through only worked because sanitization destroyed the evidence first. CHANGELOG + design §6.7/§6.10 + this entry now state it honestly; BOTH directions pinned by tests (keyword-adjacent POSIX path → rejects; non-keyword home path → masks + writes, the common C4 case).
Minors also fixed: `process.exitCode` reset in cli-remember-rich.test.js (the exit-code change made in-process error calls mutate process-global state); tasks.md Door-4→Door-5 label. Logged not fixed (out of scope, kit-internal callers only, named in design §6.10's scope note): `memoryWrite`'s `replace` action runs no privacy-strip/mask at all — trigger: any CLI/MCP verb ever reaches `replace`, or evidence of a `<private>` span landing via anti-pattern/persona supersede.

_Relates D-337 (the finding + settled direction), D-338 (closed), Task 70.4 (the screen restored), ADR-0019/Task 148 (the mask layer that introduced the ordering), the composition-verification rule (7th instance of the separately-correct-jointly-broken class — and the catalog drift is the 8th), Task 231, D-344._

## 2026-07-15 — D-343: ISSUE (recall adherence) — a fresh session answered "what did we ship today?" from `git log`, NOT from the kit's memory/DECISION-LOG (the recall trigger did not fire despite the injected snapshot + the UserPromptSubmit hint)

**Observed live in a brand-new session** (the wedge dogfood test right after the v0.5.4 work). Asked *"what did we ship today?"* — a textbook memory question — the fresh agent went **straight to `git log`** and reconstructed the day from commit messages. It never ran `cmk search`, never opened `context/DECISIONS.md` / the DECISION-LOG, never fired the `memory-search` skill. The answer was **correct and complete** — but ONLY because this session's commit messages were unusually detailed; that's the commit-message quality carrying it, not the kit's recall doing its job.

**Why this is a real gap, not a nitpick:** the kit's core promise is *"recall from memory FIRST, don't re-derive"* (the injected snapshot's own authority preamble + the SessionStart D3 gate). Here the opposite happened, AND both nudges were present and IGNORED: (a) the SessionStart snapshot was injected into context, and (b) the UserPromptSubmit hook printed the literal hint *"use the memory-search skill when the answer may already be recorded... do not re-read the code to reconstruct it."* The agent re-derived from git anyway. This is the **same class as the §5 D3 recall-adherence gate** (recall must FIRE, not be optional) — the cut-gate's W1/D3 checks passed on *structure/decision* questions, but "what did we ship" (a history question git can answer) is exactly where the git-shortcut out-competes memory.

**The seductive-shortcut mechanism:** when commit messages are well-written, `git log` gives a fast, correct-looking answer, so the agent is REWARDED for NOT using memory — reinforcing the wrong habit. The kit's value (curated why/how, superseded/retracted trail, cross-session decisions git doesn't hold) is invisible on a day where git happens to suffice.

**Why NOT a v0.5.4 blocker + disposition:** behavioral/LLM-judgment, not a code regression; the rename shipped fine and the answer was right. But it's a genuine recall-adherence weakness worth a named trigger. **Candidate directions (v0.5.x/v0.6.0 recall lane, with the D3/Task-75 work):** (a) strengthen the authority preamble to name history/"what did we ship/decide/change" questions as memory-first (git = verification, not source); (b) the recall skill's trigger description should fire on ship/history/changelog-shaped asks, not just "how/why is X structured"; (c) accept that git-log is a legitimate CONFIRM step but must FOLLOW a memory hit, never replace it. This joins D-336 (recall surfaced a mislabel) + D-339 (learn-loop signal) as the session's third recall-quality observation — all pointing at the same theme: the automatic/adherence path is the kit's soft spot, exactly where the D-85/D-164 "make it automatic, not agent-choice" thesis bites.

**FOLLOW-UP — a CONCRETE regression found while checking "is this a regression?" (the user's question):** yes, partially — and it's a real post-rename bug, distinct from the general adherence weakness above. **This repo's LIVE scaffolded `.claude/skills/memory-search/SKILL.md` still fired on the OLD bracket `"[claude-memory-kit] Memory available"`, while `capture-prompt.mjs:69` now emits `"[core-memory-kit] …"`** — so the skill's most EXPLICIT trigger (fire-on-this-exact-hint) referenced a string that no longer appears. The rename sweep (`b314369`) fixed the TEMPLATE (`template/.claude/skills/…` = `[core-memory-kit]` ✓) but NOT the already-scaffolded copy in this dogfood repo. **Root cause of the silent staleness — `installTier` (install.mjs:214-217) SKIPS any file that already exists** (`if (existsSync(targetAbs)) { skipped.push; continue }` — documented intent, install.mjs:23/340 "Never overwrites existing files", to protect user edits). So **`cmk install` on an UPDATED kit never refreshes an existing skill** — every already-installed user keeps their pre-rename skill trigger after updating, and **`cmk doctor` HC-9 checks the CLAUDE.md marker version but NOT skills, so the drift is SILENT** (HC-9 reads green over a stale skill — the exact false-green that hid it here). **Fixed in this repo:** re-synced the live skill from the corrected template (diff was exactly the 2 old-name lines; now identical to template, 0 old-name refs). **BLAST-RADIUS CORRECTION (don't overstate it — I initially wrote "every user," which is WRONG):** the SHIPPING template is CORRECT — `template/.claude/skills/…` AND `packages/cli/template/.claude/skills/…` both have 0 old-name refs (the rename sweep fixed what ships). So **a NEW install of `core-memory-kit@0.5.4` scaffolds the correct `[core-memory-kit]` trigger.** The ONLY affected case is **update-IN-PLACE**: someone who scaffolded the skill under the OLD name and then updated the kit — install skips their existing (gitignored, `.claude/`) skill, so it stays stale. Narrower than "every user," but real for anyone who upgrades rather than fresh-installs. **Also note `.claude/` is gitignored** — the skill is runtime state, so this repo's fix is LOCAL-only (corrects THIS machine's recall; not a committed change; the committed template was already right).

**The GENERAL gap (deferred, v0.5.x — narrowed to update-in-place):** kit updates that change a versioned scaffold file (skill trigger, hook contract) don't propagate to an existing install because `installTier` skips existing files, and doctor doesn't detect it — options: (a) a `cmk install --resync-skills` / `cmk repair --skills` that overwrites kit-OWNED scaffold files (skills are kit-authored, not user-data like MEMORY.md — the no-overwrite rule over-applies to them); (b) extend HC-9/doctor to detect a scaffold-file version behind the binary (a skill/hook drift check, not just the CLAUDE.md marker); (c) version-stamp skills like the managed block so drift is detectable. This is the install-skips-existing + doctor-doesnt-check-skills composition gap — a NEW named finding worth a task. _Relates install.mjs:214 (skip-existing), install.mjs:23/340 (the no-overwrite contract), doctor HC-9 (marker-only, not skills), capture-prompt.mjs:69 (the emitted hint), the rename sweep b314369 (template-only fix, correctly), D-336 + D-339 (sibling recall findings), D-343._

## 2026-07-15 — D-341: ROOT CAUSE (investigation) — the SonarCloud `opendir 'C:/proj/context'` crash is the A3S security-taint context-collection feature (server-side flag), unreachable by SIX repo-side config levers (Task 224; supersedes D-324's give-up AND my own wrong mid-investigation theories)

**The user rejected D-324's "known pre-existing crash — live with it" and drove a real investigation. It took SIX disproven theories to reach the evidence — recording ALL of them so no one re-runs a dead end.**

**Wrong theories, each disproven by EXPERIMENT (not argument) — several were mine, caught by the user:**
1. ❌ *"Stale path in a tracked file"* (D-324's approach) — `git log --all -S "C:/proj/context"` returns ONLY my own diagnosis commits; the string is in no source/config/lcov/tsconfig, in all of git history.
2. ❌ *"Automatic Analysis left ON, colliding with CI"* (my first D-341 claim, since deleted) — the settings API shows `sonar.autoscan.enabled` **value = `"false"`**. I read its mere PRESENCE as "on" WITHOUT checking the value; the UI confirmed OFF. The user's "did you check the value?" caught it. This is the "did you check the primary source?" rule failing on my own evidence.
3. ❌ *"Stale server-side architecture manifest"* — settings API has NO architecture/sources keys set; log says `A3S collection configuration fetched from API and cached: false`.
