4. ❌ *"Incremental sensor cache replaying the path"* — passed `-Dsonar.analysisCache.enabled=false`; log STILL said `Sensor cache enabled` (server ignores the flag) AND the crash persisted.
5. ❌ *"WebSensor choking on a YAML/HTML/CSS file"* — verbose: `1 language detected in 122 preprocessed files` = JS only, ZERO web files; emptying `sonar.{html,web,css,yaml}.file.suffixes` changed nothing.
6. ❌ *"Pure-JS-no-tsconfig → whole-root TS-program walk"* (the JS/TS DOC'S OWN documented pure-JS remedy) — added a minimal `tsconfig.sonar.json` + `sonar.typescript.tsconfigPaths`; crash persisted identically. **This is the one that proves it's not our config: we applied the vendor's official fix for our exact project shape and it did nothing.**

**The actual mechanism (verbose feature-flag dump, scan-log line 800):** the analyzer's `FeatureFlagResponse` for this project carries `security-stl-taint-squad-enable-a3s-context-collection=true` + `a3s-allow-common-context-collection=true`. **"A3S context collection" is the SECURITY TAINT engine's cross-file context feature** (`security-stl-taint-squad`), server-side **feature-flagged ON** for the project (rolled out ~2026-07-11 12:42 — the exact crash-start). The JS bridge's `analyzeProject` receives this A3S context and `opendir`s `C:/proj/context` inside the closed `server.cjs` runtime. Because the trigger is a **SonarSource feature flag**, not anything the scanner reads from our repo, **no repo-side config reaches it** — which is precisely why all six levers failed. `C:/proj/context` is almost certainly a placeholder/default path in the newly-rolled A3S collector (a Windows-shaped path resolved against the Linux workspace) — a vendor bug.

**Verdict on "is our config wrong for the new version / are we doing it wrong?" (the user's questions):** NO — checked against the authoritative JS/TS/CSS doc: `sonar.sources`, LCOV path, `sonar.sourceEncoding=UTF-8`, exclusions are all valid; the doc's own pure-JS remedy (#6) was TESTED and did not help. Repo is 122 clean JS files, correctly scoped. **This is a genuine SonarJS `13.2.0.43154` + A3S-context-collection regression, server-side-triggered, unreachable from the repo.**

**Resolution (honest, no false "fixed"):**
- (a) The ONLY real fix is **SonarSource's**: report the A3S-context-collection `opendir 'C:/proj/context'` regression (plugin `javascript@13.2.0.43154`, flag `enable-a3s-context-collection`, Linux runner, 122 JS files / 0 web files, six config levers proven not to reach it) to SonarCloud community/support with the verbose log. **Not done here — it's the maintainer's outward action.**
- (b) Interim: SonarCloud is ADVISORY — the REAL gates (CI test+coverage, CodeQL, gitleaks, osv-scanner, npm-audit) are green; the red is cosmetic, blocks no merge/publish (v0.5.4 published green). This is NOT the D-324 lazy-framing rerun: the difference is we now have the mechanism + a filed vendor bug, not a shrug.
- (c) Possible server-side mitigation the maintainer MAY try (unverified): disable the project's **Advanced Security / taint-analysis** feature in the SonarCloud UI (that carries the A3S context flag). Distinct from the D-39 hotspots red.

**Process lessons (both mine, both caught by the user):** (1) "advisory so a red is acceptable" (D-324) is lazy-framing — a standing red trains everyone to ignore the tool. (2) BUT the antidote is EVIDENCE, not a confident WRONG root cause — I asserted "Automatic Analysis is ON" from a setting's presence without reading its value, and "stale manifest"/"sensor cache" without testing; each cost a wrong fix the user had to catch ("did you check the value?", "are you sure our config is correct?", "maybe we're doing it wrong?", "did you check the docs?"). **Rule reinforced: name the mechanism from primary sources AND disprove-by-experiment before writing "root cause."** All temp diagnostic commits (verbose args, tsconfig.sonar.json, the wrong architecture-disable comment) reverted; `sonar-project.properties` + Task 224 updated to the evidence-backed state. _Relates D-324 (superseded), D-39 (the distinct hotspots red), Task 224, the six disproven levers, the feature-flag log evidence (line 800), D-341._

## 2026-07-15 — D-340: FIX + PROCESS LESSON — a repo RENAME must rename the GitHub repo BEFORE the publish tag, or provenance publish fails E422 (v0.5.4 publish)

**v0.5.4 published on the second attempt.** The first `git push origin v0.5.4` ran `publish.yml` through `npm ci` ✓ + `npm test` ✓ (full suite green on the tag) + release-notes ✓, then **FAILED at the provenance-publish step with npm E422**:
> `Error verifying sigstore provenance bundle: Failed to validate repository information: package.json: "repository.url" is "git+https://github.com/LH8PPL/core-memory-kit.git", expected to match "https://github.com/LH8PPL/claude-memory-kit" from provenance`

**Root cause — outward-step ORDERING, not a code bug.** npm provenance (`--provenance`) cross-checks `package.json`'s `repository.url` against the GitHub repo the Action is ACTUALLY running in. The rename set `package.json` → `core-memory-kit.git`, but the GitHub repo was still named `claude-memory-kit` at tag time (the repo rename was queued as a later outward step). Sigstore saw package-says-`core-memory-kit` vs running-repo-is-`claude-memory-kit` → refused to sign. **The publish did NOT go through** (E422 = atomic reject; `npm view` → E404, version not burned), so it was cleanly recoverable.

**The fix (the correct ordering):** rename the GitHub repo FIRST (`LH8PPL/claude-memory-kit` → `LH8PPL/core-memory-kit`) → `git remote set-url origin …/core-memory-kit.git` → `gh run rerun <id>` (the tag already points at the right commit; do NOT re-tag). Second run: all green — published `@lh8ppl/core-memory-kit@0.5.4` with provenance + GitHub Release. Verified: `npm view … version` → 0.5.4, `repository.url` matches, release `v0.5.4` exists.

**The reusable rule (for any future rename that changes `repository.url` under provenance publishing):** the GitHub-repo rename is NOT a "later" cleanup step — it is a **hard prerequisite of the publish tag**. Correct order: (1) rename the GitHub repo, (2) repoint the local remote, (3) THEN push the version tag. Doing the rename after the tag costs a failed publish + a rerun. **This is the D-84 "provenance is a composition check" instance:** `package.json` and the live repo identity are two surfaces that must COMPOSE at publish time; each was internally correct, the mismatch was the bug. _Relates the cut-gate "outward steps" ordering, ADR-0021/Task 195 (the rename), publish.yml (`--provenance`), D-335, D-340._

## 2026-07-15 — D-339: NOTE (learn-loop observe-phase signal quality) — a SUCCESSFUL cold-open net-DAMPENS the persona facts it correctly recalled (v0.5.4 cut-gate §6/E3; v0.5.3 [194] behavior, NOT rename; observe-only so no live harm)

**Found in the v0.5.4 cut-gate §6 (E3, the cold-open learn-loop gate).** The §6 cold-open was a textbook SUCCESS — a brand-new project (`cut-gate-coldopen-23`), Claude recalled the cross-project persona (uv/ruff/venv + layered-backend philosophy) from the user tier BEFORE touching the repo and scaffolded exactly the right shape (E1 PASS, the wedge). E3's signal logs fired correctly with no manual command: `recall.log` has both `source:"inject"` (SessionStart snapshot ids) and `source:"search"` (the in-chat `mk_search` query+ids); `trust-signals.log` carries deltas — the loop observed the turn. **But the DELTA DIRECTION is questionable:** the Stop-hook batch emitted **8 reinforce then 24 dampen (32 total, all applied:true, 0 quarantined)** — each of the 8 recalled persona facts got 1 reinforce then ~3 dampens, NET-LOWERING facts that were used CORRECTLY (e.g. `U-2SAAJFBH`: reinforced to 0.7 → dampened to 0.25; `U-2ANUZVSG`: 0.7 → 0.40). On a session where the persona was recalled AND correctly applied, the net signal should be reinforce, not 3:1 dampen.

**Why burst-hold didn't catch it (working per-spec, but the spec's threshold is the issue):** D-287's burst-hold rule quarantines further dampens at *">80% dampens among ≥10 same-day applied signals."* Here dampens are 24/32 = **75%** — just UNDER the 80% gate — so burst-hold correctly did nothing, and the rate-limit (max 5/fact/day) wasn't hit either (max 4/fact). So no guard misfired; the raw emit pattern itself is the concern. The likely mechanism: the new session's captures (the items-CRUD scaffold facts) register as CONTRADICTION/SUPERSESSION against the recalled persona facts (dampen-the-existing / dampen-the-old per trust-signal.mjs:6-8), firing multiple dampens per recalled fact, while only one RE-SURFACE reinforce lands each. The dampen emitters outnumber the reinforce emitter by design today (D-287: "all 7 production emitters send dampen; no production reinforce until 192's judge") — so ANY active session mechanically out-dampens its reinforces.

**Why this is NOT a v0.5.4 blocker and NOT a rename regression:** (a) the learn-loop is **OBSERVE-ONLY in v0.5.0+** — `trust_score` is an internal column with NO inject/search ranking effect yet (D-238; inject+sweep still rank on the committed `trust` ENUM), so a wrong-direction score does ZERO live harm this release; it's telemetry. (b) The behavior is pure v0.5.3 [194] code (`e554cb6`) — verified the rename commit `b314369` produced an EMPTY diff on `trust-signal.mjs` (no logic line changed; the sweep only relabeled strings elsewhere). Scores stayed in sane bounds (0.25–0.95, no floor-break, no absurd values, no storm-freeze).

**Why it MATTERS anyway (the release-gate hook):** the cut-gate guide names E3's `trust-signals.log` as *"the Task-194 release-gate artifact — v0.5.1 ranking is approved only on real, sensible deltas (no false-positives, no storms)."* THIS is that gate doing its job: before the learn-loop's scores are allowed to RANK recall (the v0.6.0 day-one-memory surface), the observe-phase data must show sane deltas — and a successful cold-open net-dampening its own correctly-used persona is exactly the false-positive signal that must be understood/fixed BEFORE ranking goes live. **Action: this is a GO-NO-GO input for the learn-loop ranking activation, not a v0.5.4 item.** Parked with a named trigger: *before any release that lets `trust_score` influence inject/search ranking, resolve whether a correct-recall session should net-reinforce (likely: RE-SURFACE of an injected+used fact should outweigh the incidental CONTRADICTION dampens from same-session captures, OR contradiction-dampen should not fire when the "contradiction" is a NEW fact on a different topic rather than a real conflict).* _Relates ADR-0017 (the learn-loop), Task 194 (`e554cb6`, the Phase-2 signal code), D-287 (burst-hold + the dampen-only-emitters posture), D-238 (observe-only, ranks on the ENUM not the score), the §6/E3 gate, D-339._

## 2026-07-15 — D-338: BUG (minor) — invalid `--shape` rejects the write but EXITS 0 (v0.5.4 cut-gate §4e/TV1; pre-existing, not rename)

**Found in the v0.5.4 cut-gate §4e (TV1).** `cmk remember 'x' --shape Banana …` correctly **refuses to write** (no fact file created) and prints `shape: must be one of State/Event/Plan/Relationship/Preference/Absence/Timeless` — but **exits 0**. The guide's TV1 spec says the invalid shape is "rejected (non-zero exit, no file written)". The SAFETY half is correct (nothing written); only the **exit code** is wrong — a script doing `cmk remember … --shape X || handle-error` would see success and not detect the rejection. **Pre-existing (Task 66, not touched by the rename); not a v0.5.4 blocker.** Fix: the shape-validation reject path in `memory-write.mjs` should set `process.exitCode = 2` like the Poison_Guard reject does. Parked as a v0.5.x polish item. _Relates Task 66 (shape field), D-338._

## 2026-07-15 — D-337: BUG (security, composition-class) — the invisible-unicode Poison_Guard is DEFEATED on the default `cmk remember` path because `maskPii` strips the char BEFORE the guard runs (v0.5.4 cut-gate §4d/PR5; pre-existing since ADR-0019, NOT a rename regression)

**Found in the v0.5.4 cut-gate §4d (PR5).** `cmk remember "<text with a real U+200B zero-width space>"` **writes the fact and exits 0** — the Task-70.4 invisible-unicode guard did NOT fire. Verified the U+200B rides argv intact (`[...text].some(c=>c.charCodeAt(0)===0x200b)` → true at the Node layer), so it is not a shell-stripping false test.

**Root cause — a textbook "separately-correct-jointly-broken" composition bug (the CLAUDE.md composition-verification class):**
- `checkPoisonGuard(<zwsp text>)` in ISOLATION correctly returns `{rejected:true, pattern_id:"injection_invisible_unicode"}` (proven directly).
- BUT on the default P/U-tier write path (`privacy.screen=on`, the default since ADR-0019/Task 148), `memory-write.mjs` runs **`maskPii(privacyStripped)` BEFORE `runPoisonGuard`** (lines ~298→314). `maskPii` **strips U+200B** as part of its normalization (proven: `maskPii` input has-U+200B=true → output has-U+200B=false). So by the time the guard runs, the invisible char is already gone → the guard sees clean text → the write proceeds.
- Net: the invisible/zero-width/bidi guard (Task 70.4's whole point — a hidden-instruction vector that ships with `git clone`) is **unreachable on the primary capture path** whenever the privacy screen is on (the default).

**Why the suite stayed green (the unit-green ≠ works-on-real-input class, applied to ordering):** `cli-poison-guard.test.js:348` asserts `checkPoisonGuard('…​…')` **directly** — the guard function alone — never through the `cmk remember` CLI where `maskPii` runs first. A test that exercises the guard in isolation structurally cannot catch "an upstream step removes the input before the guard sees it." This is exactly the integration-test-coverage-for-cross-module-flows gap: per-module correct, composition broken.

**NOT a rename regression:** `git log` shows neither `memory-write.mjs` nor `pii-patterns.mjs` was touched by the v0.5.4 rename; the ordering was introduced by `7fe820d [148] Auto-judged privacy screen (ADR-0019)` (before that, the P-tier path was home-path-only sanitization, which doesn't strip ZWSP, so the guard DID see the char). So this is a **latent security regression from ADR-0019**, surfaced now by the cut-gate — not caused by the rename, and **not a v0.5.4 blocker** (v0.5.4 is a no-feature identity cut; the bug ships identically with or without the rename).

**Fix (deferred, needs its own task — NOT done here):** either (a) run `checkPoisonGuard` on the RAW text BEFORE `maskPii` (screen-then-mask), or (b) make `maskPii` preserve invisible chars so the guard downstream still sees them, or (c) run an invisible-unicode screen as its own pre-mask stage. Option (a) is cleanest and matches Task 70.4's intent (reject hidden-instruction vectors before any transform). Add an integration test that drives the REAL `cmk remember` CLI with a genuine U+200B and asserts exit 2 + nothing written (the missing coverage). Priority: security — should land in the next patch (v0.5.5). _Relates memory-write.mjs (maskPii→runPoisonGuard ordering), pii-patterns.mjs (`maskPii` strips U+200B), poison-guard.mjs (Task 70.4 catalog), ADR-0019 (introduced the pre-guard mask), cli-poison-guard.test.js:348 (the isolation test that masks it), the CLAUDE.md composition-verification + integration-test rules, D-337._

## 2026-07-15 — D-336: ISSUE — auto-extract confabulates the PROJECT NAME as "core-memory-kit" for an unnamed project (surfaced by the v0.5.4 rename cut-gate; NOT a rename regression)

**Found in the v0.5.4 rename cut-gate (§2–§3, live Session 1 in `C:\Temp\cut-gate23`).** The gate built a FastAPI + Claude-Agent-SDK chat app; auto-extract correctly captured 8 rich project facts (B1/B2/B9 all PASS) — but **three of them were TITLED / SLUGGED as the wrong project**:
- `project_core-memory-kit-architecture-after-sdk-rebuild.md` (P-AaH2GWBP)
- `project_core-memory-kit-model-configuration-runtime.md` (P-4JUSFQ7F)
- `project_core-memory-kit-session-authentication-mechanism.md` (P-2MC9PWVV)

The BODIES are 100% correct (the FastAPI chat, layered structure, `claude-opus-4-8`, port 8000, per-connection `ClaudeSDKClient`). Only the **title + derived slug** name the app "Core-Memory-Kit" — which it is NOT.

**Root cause — pure LLM judgment, no code path.** There is NO code that reads a project name from CLAUDE.md; the title comes entirely from the Haiku extraction judge (`title: <short Title-Case headline>`, auto-extract.mjs:342). The judge, summarizing turns about an *unnamed* app, latched onto the only proper noun in its context — **the kit's own name from the injected SessionStart memory block** (*"This project uses **core-memory-kit**…"*) — and used it as the project's name. The tell is in `extract.log`: a discarded low-trust excerpt reads *"Core-memory-kit rebuilt from Messages API to Claude Agent SDK…"* — the judge literally decided the chat app *is* core-memory-kit.

**Why this is NOT a rename regression and NOT a v0.5.4 cut-blocker:** the behavior is identical pre/post rename — pre-rename the confabulation would have said "claude-memory-kit". The rename only renamed the wrong name. v0.5.4 is a no-feature identity cut; the capture behavior is unchanged, so this ships as a pre-existing latent issue, not a gate blocker. **Impact when real:** slug-namespace pollution + cross-project recall poisoning (a `cmk search` for "core-memory-kit" on an unrelated project would surface this app's facts). Low frequency (only when a project has no self-evident name AND knowledge-dense turns discuss architecture generically).

**Candidate fix (deferred, needs design — NOT done here):** the extraction prompt should instruct the judge to NEVER name the project after the memory-system block, and prefer the repo/dir name or a generic descriptor when the project's own name is absent from the turn. Parked as a v0.5.x capture-quality candidate with a named trigger: *fix when a 2nd project exhibits the cross-project-recall poisoning, OR when the extraction-prompt is next revised.* _Relates auto-extract.mjs:342 (the title directive), the injected memory block (CLAUDE.md managed section), D-122 (the extraction-prompt class), D-336._

**UPDATE (§5 Session 2 recall, same cut-gate) — impact now CONFIRMED user-facing, strengthening the trigger.** In the live recall session, asked "how is this project structured?", the `memory-search` skill (W1/W2 both PASS — auto-fired, cited, paraphrase-recall via hybrid worked) returned an otherwise-correct answer that opened *"This is **core-memory-kit** — a single-user WebSocket chat app"* — i.e. the mislabel propagated from the auto-extract titles into a user-facing recall answer, exactly the poisoning predicted above. This is no longer a latent slug-namespace concern; it visibly names the wrong project TO THE USER. Still not a rename regression, still not a v0.5.4 blocker (identity cut, unchanged behavior), but the confirmed recall impact raises the fix's priority within the v0.5.x capture-quality lane. **Separately verified NOT a bug (same session):** the old broadcast-era fact `P-TEHVL5Q2` staying `superseded_by: null` after the SDK rebuild is EXPECTED — per-turn auto-extract is additive by design; temporal-supersede runs only in `temporal-sweep.mjs` via `weekly-curate` (auto-extract's supersede path is documented "unreachable today", auto-extract.mjs:654). Recall correctly surfaced BOTH records and flagged which is current — the honest pre-consolidation behavior, not a gap._

## 2026-07-15 — D-335: DECISION + EXECUTION — Task 195: rename `claude-memory-kit` → `core-memory-kit` (executes ADR-0012; ADR-0021)

**The user chose RENAME to `core-memory-kit`** (from the candidate pool KEEP-claude-memory-kit / RENAME-to-core-memory-kit / elevate-cmk-as-brand). The name keeps the `cmk` initialism deliberately — `cmk` binary + `MEMORY_KIT_USER_DIR` + `@lh8ppl/cmk-canonicalize` are unchanged. **ADR-0021** written as ADR-0012's successor (0012 marked superseded, content preserved).

**Execution + the decisions inside it:**
- **Config dir: a DIRECT swap** `~/.claude-memory-kit` → `~/.core-memory-kit`, NOT a migration. An earlier revision BUILT a copy-not-move / keep-the-old / marker-gated migration (12 tests green, commit `201845b`) — the right shape IF strangers depended on the old path. But the user (the SOLE real user) chose *"change everything, I'll reinstall,"* so the migration protects nobody and was removed as dead complexity. `defaultUserDir` is back to a one-liner. (Lesson preserved: a shared-tier default rename WITH real users needs copy-not-move; here there are none.)
- **Corpus sweep: 132 files**, via an auditable script (not blind sed) with per-family rules (npm name, repo URLs, instruction filenames + detector in lockstep, config-dir literal, bare prose). **Two carve-outs enforced by the script:** (1) FROZEN records + dogfood `context/` skipped entirely (`docs/adr` except 0021 / `docs/journey` / `docs/research` / `docs/conversation-log` / `docs/sources` / `archive` / `docs/process`) — they name the project as it WAS; (2) `awrshift/claude-memory-kit` (a different product's repo) preserved via negative-lookbehind. 550 ins / 550 del — a pure 1:1 rename.
- **The two-pass review caught a real miss:** the sweep rewrote a historical v0.4.0 tarball filename (`lh8ppl-claude-memory-kit-0.4.0.tgz`) inside `RESUME-HERE.md` (a dated point-in-time breadcrumb) and flattened Task 195's OWN entry in `tasks.md` (the KEEP option started reading "keep core-memory-kit" — nonsensical). Both fixed: RESUME-HERE reverted; the tasks.md entry's historical name-as-subject references corrected + flagged, pointing at the unedited DECISION-LOG/ADR-0012 as authoritative. The carve-out correctly protected the DECISION-LOG's own D-255/D-257/D-334 name-history (untouched).
- **Verified:** full suite 3050 green, 15 validators green (pack-completeness, agent-adapter-parity, doc-registry all hold under the new name), live `cmk install` + `remember` + `search` round-trip works, `defaultUserDir({})` → `~/.core-memory-kit`.

**User outward steps (not done by the assistant):** publish `@lh8ppl/core-memory-kit` + `npm deprecate @lh8ppl/claude-memory-kit "renamed to @lh8ppl/core-memory-kit"`; rename the GitHub repo `LH8PPL/claude-memory-kit` → `LH8PPL/core-memory-kit` (auto-redirects old URLs). Ships as the v0.5.4 headline. _Relates ADR-0021 (supersedes ADR-0012), Task 195, D-255/D-257/D-334 (the deferral + trigger history), the copy-migration in commit `201845b` (built-then-removed), D-335._

## 2026-07-14 — D-334: DECISION — the v0.5.3-cut minor-boundary backlog sweep (D-248/D-267): trigger-fired walk + per-task verdicts; Task 195 fired → laned v0.5.4

**The sweep (required at every minor per D-248; opens with the D-267 trigger-fired walk).** `validate-backlog-triggers` green — all 42 open top-level tasks carry a lane or named trigger. Walked every trigger asking the D-267 question ("has this condition become TRUE since the last sweep?"):

- **Task 195 (cross-agent NAME decision) — TRIGGER FIRED.** Its trigger was "re-decide after the two committed breadth agents ship (Cursor + Codex live)." Both are now live (Cursor v0.4.5, Codex v0.5.2) → the condition is met, and this is exactly the D-267 fired-but-could-go-unnoticed case the walk exists to catch. **Verdict: LANED v0.5.4** (the user's call at the sweep — commit it, don't re-defer; the name is an outward-facing identity decision that stays the user's, executed as a bake-off + ADR-0012 successor in the v0.5.4 governance batch). D-257's rename-to-general lean carries forward as the starting position, not a settled outcome.
- **Recall-research trio (149/176/178) + Task 51 — trigger already dispositioned, NOT a fired stray.** Their "next recall-surface minor" trigger reads as if v0.5.3 (a search minor) could fire it, BUT the 2026-07-12 market sweep (D-326) ALREADY re-verdicted them to **v0.6.0** explicitly (RELEASE-PLAN D-309 queue item 5 + line 131) — they ride the day-one-memory corpus surface where an ADOPT verdict has real leverage, not v0.5.3's blend work. No re-open: a settled lane, confirmed still-correct.
- **Task 199 (write-time temporal detection) — trigger's OR-clause ("v0.5 learn-loop touches the write path") did NOT fire.** The v0.5.3 batch touched the READ/search surface (blend, labels, view-gate, stats), not `writeFact`; the AND-clause (198 shipped + a live stale-State answer within the per-session window) has no new evidence either. Keep deferred, trigger intact.
- **The rest** (47/48/51/55/67/68/70/71/73/95/96/127/140/141/146/166/174/175/177/179/180/184/186/189/196-tail/208/210/217/224/225-229) — each carries a live lane (D-309 queue: v0.5.4 governance, v0.6.0 day-one, v0.7.0 team) or an un-fired named trigger; none crossed two consecutive minors without a verdict (the D-248 kill-or-commit backstop). Task 224 (SonarCloud) stays server-side/advisory. No kills this sweep.

**Net:** one fired trigger (195 → v0.5.4), zero strays, zero kills; the D-309 queue (v0.5.4 governance → v0.6.0 day-one → v0.7.0 team) stands. v0.5.3 clear to cut. _Relates D-248 (the sweep rule), D-267 (the trigger-fired-walk first step), D-309/D-326 (the version queue this confirms), D-255/D-257 (Task 195's history), D-334._

## 2026-07-14 — D-333: DECISION — Task 212 ships `cmk stats memory-health` (AutoMem's Figure-4 behavioral dashboard; closes the v0.5.3 rider batch)

**The insight (AutoMem, arXiv 2607.01224):** a small set of PROCESS metrics — not task success — makes memory-system quality observable and improvable (their optimized agents: writes-per-search −54–72%, redundant writes −68–83%, empty searches −13–50%). The kit's raw data already existed (recall.log/audit.log/truncation.log); this is AGGREGATION + a surface, no new capture, no LLM.

**Design calls:** (a) a **new `cmk stats memory-health` verb, not a doctor section** — the D-277 split applied: doctor answers "is it healthy? (pass/fail)", stats answers "how is the process behaving? (informational)"; mixing them muddies both. (b) Module named **`memory-stats.mjs`** — `memory-health.mjs` already exists (Task 144's CONTENT-quality doctor section over the fact archive); the near-collision is disambiguated in both modules' headers, the glossary, and CLI.md. (c) **v1 metric set:** writes-per-search; empty-search rate + the recovered-by-retry split (time-window join, 10 min — production search entries carry session:null); redundant-write rate (audit actions recurrence/queued/merged/temporal_supersede ÷ writes appended/created/replaced); repeated identical searches (normalized-query counts >1); snapshot cap pressure (truncation.log events + dropped sections). Current window vs the previous same-length window (`--window 7|30`, default 7), trend arrows with polarity annotated inline (down = improving for the three rates). (d) **REPORT-ONLY** (the D-169 observe-before-alarming line): no thresholds, no PASS/FAIL, no exit-code effect — pinned by tests (incl. a no-mutation Door-2 pin). (e) CLI-only v1 (a human-facing report; the `--from-file` CLI-only precedent — no MCP tool, documented).

_Relates AutoMem Fig-4 (the indicator set), Tasks 190/93 (the data sources), 194 (the consumer — design §20.7 records these as its before/after numbers), 144 (the content-quality sibling), 189 (the ROI sibling, keeps its research gate), D-277 (the verb-split precedent), D-308/D-309, D-333._

## 2026-07-13 — D-332: DECISION — Task 211 ships the query state-view gate (A-TMA's retrieval-level mechanism; the §16.18 4-view cut, un-deferred on its evidence)

**The gap:** every query was state-neutral — a history question ("what did we use before X?") had no path to PREFER the superseded/expired record (expired hidden by default; superseded competing on BM25 alone). §16.18 deferred the 7-mode classifier + reranker as infrastructure-heavy — correctly; A-TMA's new evidence (its retrieval wins come from an explicitly RULE-BASED 4-view profiler — hint words + negation guards, no classifier infra) killed the cost premise for the 4-view cut (the D-248 revisit rule, executed).

**The build:** `query-state-view.mjs` (pure, total, zero-LLM): hint catalogs (historical/transition/current phrases, word-boundary token matching) + a 5-token negation guard — a negated past-hint COUNTS AS a current hit ("not what we used before" asks for the present). Verdict precedence: transition > (past+present together = a comparison → transition) > historical > current > neutral. In search() (facts scope only): historical/transition auto-set includeExpired AND swap the FTS query for `contentQuery` — **the composition find of the task**: hint words are view METADATA the classifier consumed, and FTS5's implicit-AND would otherwise demand the asker's temporal adverb appear in fact bodies ("deploy target before" matched NOTHING until the strip; found by the integration tests going empty). Historical additionally buckets stateful (209-labeled) rows FIRST — a stable partition, never a score change (§20.3 + the 194 blend untouched; within buckets the BM25/blend order is preserved exactly). Current/neutral: byte-identical pipeline, envelope-identical too (`state_view` + the one-line note ride ONLY when the gate changed retrieval). `--state-view` (CLI) / `state_view` (mk_search, parity) are overrides only. The 7-mode + reranker version stays deferred on the original grounds — §16.18 carries the shipped-marker annotation.

_Relates A-TMA (mechanism + numbers), §16.18 (the deferral this narrows — annotated in-place), Task 209 (labels — the QA sibling; historical results arrive labeled), Task 194 (the blend it composes with but never touches), D-248 (the revisit rule), D-308/D-309, D-332._

## 2026-07-13 — D-331: DECISION — Task 209 ships state-labeled recall (A-TMA's QA-level mechanism; the v0.5.3 rider batch opens)

**The gap:** the kit COMPUTED and STORED temporal state (Task 66 windows, `superseded_by`, `expires_at`, tombstones) but never TOLD Claude at recall time — search results and the snapshot rendered facts as undifferentiated bullets, so a legitimately-surfaced old state forced Claude to infer currency from prose. A-TMA (arXiv 2607.01935, Case Study 1): identical retrieved evidence flips from wrong to correct answer with deterministic state labels + a one-line instruction alone — pure labeling, zero retrieval change.

**The build (labels, never re-ranks — §20.3 intact):**
- `state-label.mjs` (new, PURE — no LLM, no DB, injectable clock): `projectStateLabel({deletedAt, supersededBy, expiresAt, now})` → null (current — UNLABELED, the zero-noise contract) | `retracted` | `superseded` | `expired`; precedence retracted > superseded > expired; `expires_at == now` is already expired (the 66.3 exclusive-end convention). Fixed vocabulary: `[superseded — kept for history]` / `[expired]` / `[retracted]`.
- **Search**: the facts-scope SELECT gains `superseded_by`/`expires_at`; each non-current row carries `state`; the CLI prefixes the label ahead of the snippet; mk_search rides the one-line instruction as an EXTRA content block only when a labeled row is present (results stay content[0], shape-compatible — the degradedNote pattern).
- **get (shared read-core, ADR-0014)**: same projection on live rows; a recovered tombstone is labeled `retracted` explicitly.
- **Snapshot**: `labelSupersededBullets` — a pure string scan of bullet+provenance pairs BEFORE `cleanScratchpadBody` strips the comments (the only place the bullet-state signal exists); the instruction line is cap-RESERVED from raw blocks but EMITTED only if a labeled bullet survives truncation (over-reserve keeps `snapshot ≤ capBytes` strictly true, §7.1.2). A label-free snapshot is byte-identical to pre-209. Superseded is the ONLY bullet state (tombstoned bullets are stripped from the file; bullets carry no expires_at).
- **HEALTH-CHECKS.md** gains the A-TMA bank/retrieval/QA "When recall goes wrong" diagnostic (never-marked vs not-surfaced vs surfaced-but-misread — the levels fail independently; fixing the wrong one has zero effect).

**Self-caught while wiring:** the Task-194 lifecycle-map edit had introduced a duplicate edge number (two #19 rows — the pre-existing expectation→judgment row was missed); renumbered 19–22 in file order. _Relates A-TMA (the mechanism + evidence), Task 66/198 (the shipped state engine this surfaces), §20.3 (honored), Task 194 (the ranking sibling), Task 211 (the retrieval-side sibling, next), D-308/D-309, D-331._

## 2026-07-13 — D-330: DECISION — Task 194 ships the confidence-gated search blend + survival gate + anti-pattern conversion (ADR-0017 Phase 2 — the MEASURE→RETRIEVE edge CLOSED; opens v0.5.3)

**The learn-loop is now end-to-end:** `trust_score` stopped being decorative (the field-wide "inert socket" anti-pattern) and became load-bearing in the ONE place the ADR authorized — SEARCH, where the DB is already open. The design calls, each grounded:

- **Blend shape = Memoria's retrieval multiplier** (the survey's cleanest oracle-free ranking-integrated template): `blended = bm25 × (1 + λ·(trust_score − 0.5))` on FTS5's negative-better rank — multiplicative = scale-invariant vs corpus-dependent BM25 magnitudes. `BLEND_LAMBDA = 0.5` bounds the adjustment at ±22.5% (separates ties/near-ties; junk can never leapfrog a strong match — well inside Memoria's own [0.5, 2.0] clamp). Stable sort → gate-closed rows keep exact BM25 order; oversampled fetch (3×) → a dampened row SINKS, never drops (demote-not-evict at the result list). Hybrid inherits via RRF (rank-position fusion reads the pre-blended keyword list); semantic-only stays unblended (the backend owns its similarity scale — hybrid is the configured default anyway).
- **Confidence gate = a new `signal_count` feedback counter** (SYSTEM-MAP §6 named "feedback counters" in the index) incremented per APPLIED signal in `applyTrustSignal` — rate-limited/quarantined/not-found never count. Threshold `BLEND_MIN_SIGNALS = 3` (the kit's existing three-occurrences-=-a-pattern constant, ADR-0016 §20.1). **This resolves ADR-0017's open seam by construction:** recurrence/restatement lives in the `initTrustScore` SEED, never in the counter — restatement can't buy rank the way a tool-outcome does. Overlay posture (D-237): full reindex → counter resets to 0 → the gate honestly reports "no evidence" until re-earned.
- **Judgments never rank** — excluded by `judgment_*.md` source_file (the checkable rule judgment.mjs's header promised).
- **Survival gate (ExpeL prune-at-zero, demote-not-evict flavored):** an applied dampen on an ALREADY-floored fact routes a prune-candidate to `context/queues/prune-review.md` (always the project tier — where the trust overlay lives, the M6 posture) — automatic, audited, NEVER silent-delete. The queue is PRESERVATIONAL (conflict-queue convention): resolved entries keep `resolution:` markers, which double as the idempotency memory (no re-nag, ever — including a `keep`ed fact that keeps failing).
- **Anti-pattern conversion (Memento/REMEMBERER/Negative-Knowledge):** `cmk queue prune` / `mk_queue_resolve {queue:'prune'}` offer convert/forget/keep/skip. Convert retains the content reframed: a bullet is rewritten in place via memoryWrite replace (`⚠️ AVOID (repeatedly failed in practice): …` — injected where it lived); a fact file is retyped `type: anti-pattern` (a CONVERSION-ONLY type — writeFact's VALID_TYPES deliberately excludes it, the judgment posture: born from the loop, never dictated), AVOID-titled, warning-blocked, filename kept (frontmatter is truth; a rename would churn INDEX.md + git history), + a high-trust warning bullet in MEMORY.md § Anti-patterns (the injection half). Forget is shape-aware (`forgetCandidate`): fact → `forget()` tombstone; bullet → memoryWrite remove tombstone (found by TDD — `forget()` answers not-found for bullets, and the first resolver draft mis-counted that as success).
- **§20.3 amended, not reversed** (the ADR's pre-authorized narrow revision, recorded with the evidence): the hot-path rule stands — inject enum-ordered, no index-db import, structurally pinned by a regression test on inject-context's import graph. design §20.7 is the canonical mechanics section; §9.3 cross-references.
- **Live gate automated (D-313 posture):** the Done-when cold-open — real `cmk remember` ×2 → real `cmk search` → dampen one fact through the loop's own gate → the real bin ranks the healthy fact first — runs as a REAL-bin spawn test in `tests/cli-search-blend.test.js`, not a deferred manual step. (Found live: `cmk reindex` rebuilds INDEX.md, not the search DB — the search DB populates via the reader's `reindexBoot`, so the test warms via a real search. The automatic-path criterion, demonstrated.)

Surfaces: `search.mjs` (blend), `index-db.mjs` (signal_count column + migration), `trust-signal.mjs` (counter + gate), `prune-queue.mjs` + `anti-pattern.mjs` (new), `subcommands.mjs` (`cmk queue prune`), `mcp-server.mjs` (queue-tool parity). Tests: `cli-search-blend.test.js` (16, incl. the live real-bin gate + the §20.3 pin) + `cli-prune-queue.test.js` (14, five doors + over-mutation). _Relates ADR-0017 (Decisions #1/#3/#5 + Consequences), ADR-0016 §20.1/20.2, D-237/D-238 (overlay + sweep verdicts — both still hold), D-252/D-308/D-309 (the lane), the 2026-07-01 failure-learning survey (Memoria/memclaw/ExpeL), D-313 (live-test parity), D-330._

## 2026-07-13 — D-329: FIX — Task 218 becomes CLI↔MCP PARITY: the MCP reader skipped the per-query index refresh EVERY CLI read does; + a full parity audit closed 4 more drifts (the user's "make them the same")

**Final resolution: the MCP server was the ONE reader that didn't run the per-query incremental `reindexBoot` the CLI's `withReadDb` runs before every read.** Indexing is lazy/reader-driven — `cmk remember` writes the fact FILE; the FTS index only refreshes when a READER reindexes (the CLI does this in `withReadDb`; `cmk search` at subcommands.mjs:1168). The MCP read tools skipped it, so a fact written after boot was invisible to `mk_search`/`mk_get`/`mk_timeline`/`mk_recent_activity`. **Fix = a shared `refreshIndexForRead()` in mcp-server.mjs, called at the top of all four index-backed read tools — bringing the MCP surface to CLI parity.** Deterministic (a synchronous inline refresh, no async FS event to lose), load-immune, ~0ms when nothing changed (the `files` mtime/sha1 checkpoint short-circuits). `mk_cite` correctly skips it (pure string op, no db — matches `runCite`); the queue-list tools skip it (queues are file-backed, not index-backed).

**PIVOT — the WATCHER approach was tried and REJECTED on evidence (preserved per the decision-trail rule).** The first fix wired the fully-implemented-but-never-called `startRuntimeWatcher` (chokidar, design §9.2) into `runMcpServer`, with ordered async teardown; skill-review even found + fixed a real `handleUnlink` crash-guard gap the wiring newly activated. But it **failed 5/5 under the full concurrent suite** — chokidar drops/delays native FS events on Windows when every core is saturated (the user's "always-busy laptop" case; chokidar #417). Deadline-tuning didn't help (still 5/5) because it's not a timing bug. **Deep research settled it** ([2026-07-13-sqlite-reader-freshness.md](../research/2026-07-13-sqlite-reader-freshness.md), 17-project field survey + SQLite primary sources + 3 repros): (a) the staleness is NOT a WAL/snapshot issue — a fresh autocommit `.all()` sees committed data; the REAL cause is lazy/reader-driven indexing (the writer never commits to the db, so `PRAGMA data_version` stays 2→2 and even a fresh connection sees 0 until a reader reindexes); (b) the field consensus is **re-read/re-derive per request** (official MCP memory server, basic-memory NullPool, the kit's own CLI) — almost nobody keeps a long-lived reader + a watcher, and watchers are only ever re-ingest triggers, always backstopped, never the freshness guarantee. So the watcher was the wrong shape; the per-query refresh (what the CLI already does) is the right one. The `handleUnlink` try/catch guard was KEPT (a genuine robustness improvement to `startRuntimeWatcher` regardless); the watcher itself stays implemented-but-unused.

**Then the user's "check ALL paths — could be a good reason, but we need them the same": a full CLI↔MCP parity audit (read + write, 7 axes).** It confirmed the read-refresh fix covers every index-backed read, and found the drift class was broader — 5 more param asymmetries. Verdicts:
- **FALSE ALARM:** `mk_remember` terse trust — `memoryWrite` defaults `trust ?? 'high'`, so MCP terse already writes high like the CLI. No drift.
- **FIXED (mechanical):** `cmk trust` omitted `userDir` (relied on the library-level `defaultUserDir()` homedir-reach the code warns against) → now threads `resolveUserDir()` like `runForget`/`runLessonsPromote`; matches `mk_trust`.
- **FIXED (params added to MCP, the user's call):** `mk_search` gained `include_expired` (the user's decision — the AGENT can opt into facts past a DECLARED `expires_at`; distinct from tombstones, which stay agent-blind per D-163 — "expired" = a user-set shelf-life, NOT age; an undated old fact always recalls); `mk_lessons_promote` gained `section`; `mk_forget` gained `deleted_by`. All CLI-parity, additive, low-risk, doc'd in MCP.md.
- **INTENTIONAL (documented, NOT changed):** mk_get/mk_search tombstone-blindness (D-163); `actor: mcp-user-explicit` provenance; MCP soft-DoS caps (get ids .max(100), timeline depth .max(50), remember text .max(5000)); `cites` accepted-but-rejected (§16.39); `--from-file`/`--json` CLI-only (D-81 shell safety); `merge-both` CLI-only (interactive composition, mcp-server.mjs:538); forget CLI-`--yes` vs MCP two-step token; `--project` CLI-only (Kiro no-`cd` case).

**Tests:** `cli-mcp-freshness.test.js` (deterministic — external `cmk remember`/`cmk forget` → the next `mk_search`/`mk_get` sees it; verified fails when the fix is stashed = no false-green) + 3 new parity tests in `cli-mcp-server.test.js` (include_expired hides-by-default/reveals-on-opt-in; section override wins; deleted_by in the tombstone provenance). _Relates the CLI `withReadDb` (subcommands.mjs, the parity source), design §9.2, the [reader-freshness research](../research/2026-07-13-sqlite-reader-freshness.md), the CLI↔MCP parity audit, `startRuntimeWatcher` (tried + rejected, kept unused with the handleUnlink guard), ADR-0014 (CLI/MCP parity), D-163 (tombstone-blindness), the 2026-07-10 recall review (finding 1), D-312, D-329._

## 2026-07-13 — D-328: DECISION — Task 165 resolves to DOCUMENT-not-build (the kiro-cli MCP prompt is an intended Kiro platform limitation) + (b) was already shipped; closes 165

**165(a) — the kiro-cli MCP approval prompt (D-285's parked root cause).** The user asked for research before a fix ("did you actually go online — articles, docs, forums?"). A primary-source pass (real WebFetch/WebSearch, verified in-session by me too) settled it:
- **kiro-cli IGNORES `mcp.json`'s `autoApprove` BY DESIGN** — [Kiro issue #4672](https://github.com/kirodotdev/Kiro/issues/4672) (**Closed** = intended, not a bug); that field is IDE-only. The CLI's only prompt-free MCP path is a custom agent's `allowedTools: ["@server"]`.
- **You CANNOT pre-trust tools for the built-in `kiro_default` agent** — [#4384](https://github.com/kirodotdev/Kiro/issues/4384) (**Open**): a reporter wrote `~/.kiro/agents/kiro_default.json` with `allowedTools` and "the fetch tool remains untrusted." So there is no project-trust key to wire (Option 3 is impossible).
- **The kit already does the maximal correct thing:** it guard-sets `cmk` as the CLI default (when none exists) → cmk-active → explicit memory runs through pre-trusted `cmk` SHELL commands (the D-198 `includeMcpJson:false` design — MCP tools don't wire to custom kiro-cli agents anyway per #5873/#5662, so loading MCP would give dead tools + a console popup for zero benefit). So `allowedTools:["@cmk"]` on the cmk agent would do NOTHING (it has no MCP) — the half-Option-1 the research suggested is a no-op HERE.
- **The residual prompt hits ONLY a user who kept their OWN pre-existing default agent** (kit reports `skipped-existing`) → they land on `kiro_default`/their agent → MCP save prompts once/session. Automatic CAPTURE is unaffected (project `.kiro/hooks/` fire on any agent, D-285).
- **Verdict: not a kit bug — an intended Kiro CLI limitation; DOCUMENT it + sharpen the install hint.** Fix = the KIRO.md trust note (cites #4672/#4384) + the install `skipped-existing` message now gives the article's canonical remedies (`kiro-cli agent set-default cmk` / `/tools trust @cmk` per session) instead of the older `kiro-cli --agent cmk`. No re-wire.
- **Source (corroborating, the user handed me the raw article):** Carlos Biagolini, "How to Configure Tool Auto-Approval in Kiro CLI" (awstip.com, 2026-05-04) — confirms the `@server-name` auto-approve pattern, `agent set-default`, the session flags (`--trust-all-tools` / `/tools trust`), and `useLegacyMcpJson:true` as the load-MCP switch; all its examples work by making the custom agent the default, saying nothing about trusting the built-in default — reinforcing the verdict.

**165(b) — the IDE delete-guardrail — ALREADY SHIPPED.** The parked "(b) IDE has no PreToolUse, can't wire a delete-guard" premise was overturned by the D-200 research (the IDE exposes 10 triggers incl. PreToolUse) and IMPLEMENTED in the D-182 IDE-hooks rework: `kiro-ide-hooks.mjs` writes `cmk-guard.json` with `trigger: 'PreToolUse'` (can BLOCK on non-zero exit) — the code comment literally reads "supersedes Task 165(b)." Nothing to build.

**META (the process win):** the user's "did you actually go online?" was the primary-source rule applied to my own research claim — and when I fetched the sources myself in-session, one even sharpened (#4672 is Closed, strengthening "intended"). The user then handed the paywalled article directly (the redirect chain dead-ended at Medium's login wall), which corroborated without changing the verdict. _Relates D-285 (the parked root cause this closes), D-198 (the includeMcpJson:false design), D-200/D-182 (the IDE-hooks rework that shipped (b)), Task 165, D-328._

## 2026-07-12 — D-327: DECISION — the Codex adapter ships on the generic seam (hooks-json+MCP), NOT as the plugin-marketplace build the June-20 research scoped out

**The re-verification that changed the plan:** Task 196's Codex leg was classified `plugin-marketplace` ("highest effort, lowest reuse — out of v0.4.0 scope") by the 2026-06-20 seam research. The build-time primary-source pass ([the research note](../research/2026-07-12-codex-adapter-surfaces.md)) found that classification OBSOLETE: Codex shipped a first-class hooks system (UserPromptSubmit 2026-03-18; Pre/PostToolUse ~v0.117; the installed binary is 0.142.5) whose events AND response envelope are near byte-compatible with Claude Code's (`hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext` / `permissionDecision: deny` / `tool_name: "Bash"`). **Codex rides the Task-50 generic seam** — a pure-data `defineAgentProfile` + two small mechanism branches — exactly the patch-sized shape the seam promised.

**The three Codex-specific decisions:** (1) **MCP via `codex mcp add`, never TOML surgery** — Codex's MCP config is TOML (`~/.codex/config.toml`), the project-level file is trusted-only with open Desktop issues (#13025/#13056), and the kit has no TOML writer; the agent's own CLI is the safe path (live-verified 0.142.5; off-PATH binary → the install prints the manual one-liner — the Desktop app bundles codex.exe off-PATH). (2) **Turn capture reads the rollout file** — Codex's `Stop` payload is status-only, but every hook payload carries `transcript_path`; `readCodexTurn` parses the rollout jsonl (format pinned from a REAL live capture, committed as a fixture per the Task-221 corpus discipline). (3) **The hash-trust honesty line** — Codex SKIPS non-managed hooks until the user runs `/hooks` once; every doc + the install output says so (never claim zero-step; the Kiro one-time-trust class).

**Also:** `codex exec --skip-git-repo-check -s read-only --json -` (stdin prompt) is the Task-200 backend leg — live-verified end-to-end on the maintainer's machine (~5s round-trip, JSONL `item.completed`/`agent_message` parse), far faster than cursor-agent's 60–83s. The uninstall's `codex mcp remove` is gated on project-side install evidence (a clean project stays a quiet no-op — the user-level registration must not be yanked by an unrelated project's uninstall). _Relates Task 196 (the tail), Task 50 (the seam), Task 200/201 (the backend + split-brain), D-270..D-278 (the backend lineage), the 2026-06-20 seam note (superseded classification, preserved per the decision-trail rule), D-327._

**The input (the user's full-read directive):** three sources, all published 2026-07-10, read in full — the origin creator's v3 video ("I Rebuilt Hermes's Best Feature in Claude Code", transcript + 20 slides + the free build plan), LangChain's OpenWiki Brains announcement, and the MLM memory-taxonomy decision-tree article. Findings + gap map: [the sweep note](../research/2026-07-12-day-one-memory-market-sweep.md). ## 2026-07-12 — D-325: DECISION — v0.5.2 scope = the cross-agent breadth patch (Cursor + Codex adapters + 165 + 218); Cursor interactive gate flagged pending-tokens
